title: Business & Marketing Associate
description: Please note: This position is not eligible for employment permit sponsorship. Candidates must have the right to work in Ireland at the time of application. **About us** Future Direct is a dynamic and rapidly expanding consultancy firm specialising in Irish immigration services, serving a diverse range of B2B and B2C clients globally. We take immense pride in the life\-changing nature of our work, and we offer our team a culture of continuous learning, genuine career progression, and a supportive environment where your contributions make a real difference. **The role** We are looking for an energetic and versatile Business \& Marketing Associate to join our growing team in Dublin. This is a hybrid role, approximately 70% focused on marketing and branding, with 30% supporting business operations and is well suited to someone who enjoys wearing multiple hats in a fast\-paced, purpose\-driven environment. As Future Direct continues to grow, this role has the potential to evolve and branch out in line with business needs, offering the right candidate the opportunity to grow with us. **What you will do** **Marketing \& Branding** * Plan and execute content across social media platforms including LinkedIn, Instagram, and others * Support the development and maintenance of brand identity and visual assets * Drive marketing campaigns, email marketing, and the production of promotional materials such as leaflets and brochures * Identify and coordinate attendance at relevant industry events and networking opportunities * Produce written content including blogs, articles, and promotional copy * Support website management, SEO initiatives, and AI\-driven search optimisation **Business \& Administration** * Provide general administrative support across departments as needed * Assist with internal coordination, scheduling, and documentation * Handle invoicing and follow up on outstanding payments in a timely manner * Assist with month\-end accounts administration and financial record\-keeping **What we are looking for** * A degree or qualification in Marketing, Digital Marketing, Communications, or a related field * At least 1 year of experience in a marketing, branding, or communications role * Basic knowledge or experience of bookkeeping or accounts administration is a plus * A genuine team player who collaborates well across functions * Strong critical thinking and problem\-solving skills * A proactive, can\-do attitude with a willingness to take ownership * Good written communication skills , particularly for content and blog writing

title: Business Analyst - Data & Back-Office Systems - based in Luxembourg
description: **This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence.** The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi\-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support. The **EIB** , the European Union's bank, is seeking to recruit for its Finance Directorate (FI), Planning and Settlement of Operations Department (PRO), Contracts and Business Solutions Division (CBS), Business Digitalisation and Support Unit (BuDiS) at its headquarters in Luxembourg, a **Business Analyst – Data \& Back\-Office Systems\*.** **This is a full\-time position at grade 5 for which the EIB offers a permanent contract.** * Internal benchmark Officer Business Support for IT Applications ***Panel interviews are anticipated for June 2026\.*** Purpose You will lead and structure complex business analysis activities to implement and maintain critical software solutions. This role involves providing expertise in business requirements analysis, including data\-driven insights, process analysis, solution design and software configuration to meet the organization's digitalization, technology security, and regulatory needs. Operating Network You will report to the Head of Unit (HoU). You will be in contact with other colleagues in FI (Finance Directorate) and other Directorates for business aspects, while you will co\-operate closely with the other units of the FI\-PRO Back\-Office Divisions and with GIS on technical aspects. Regular telework is allowed if the Bank’s telework policy and the Minimum Presence Required rules applicable for FI PRO teams are respected. Accountabilities **BUSINESS PROJECTS** Provide business support to projects with specific scope and timing to ensure their realization within the set scope, time, and budget. * Act as a Business Analyst on complex business or IT projects in the Lending, Treasury, or Borrowings area (e.g. new product implementation, new functionality introduction, new application, Package upgrades, etc.). * Collaborate in workshops/discussions to understand – within assigned business area – project goals, workflows, complex business needs, gaps, and opportunities. * Review the technical and transactional documentation related to the approved solution before implementation. * Advise the project stakeholders on solution feasibility, risks, and implementation trade\-offs. * Report on a regular basis the status of the FI\-PRO applications’ developments to the HoU. **PROVIDE SUPPORT AND ENSURE MAINTENANCE AND DEVELOPMENT OF SYSTEMS** Ensure efficient deployment and operation of FI\-PRO’s applications within the unit, maintaining reliable financial data and high user satisfaction. * Provide 1st level user support: analyse requests, identify workarounds, provide specific advice and follow\-up on resolutions. * Identify recurring issues and propose structural improvements to processes, data flows and reporting. * Gather user requirements for maintenance and developments, follow\-up on implementations. * Execute User Acceptance Tests (UAT) test plans to ensure that business requirements and functional specifications are implemented properly and meet objectives, (e.g. review and definition of rules, reports and activities). * Provide status updates to the senior management on the development. * Collaborate to the training sessions organization when required. **CONTACT POINT** Manage relationships and collaborate with counterparts from others EIB Group’s Directorates. * Act as the contact point and represent FI\-PRO in working groups and transversal initiatives, when related to the applications under unit’s remit. * Participate and collaborate to NPCs and working groups that impact FI\-PRO on new products or bank\-wide initiatives. Define solutions that will meet the requirements of the services and cover the business needs. * Streamline the communications with IT and the software vendor for problem resolution. **Qualifications** * University degree (minimum an equivalent to a Bachelor) preferably in the fields of information technology, mathematics or finance. * Minimum 5 years of relevant professional experience, ideally in the financial sector, or business consultant companies. Solid proven experience as Business analyst. * Proven experience in data visualization tools (ideally 2 years or more), particularly Power BI, including designing business\-facing dashboards, defining semantic data models, and translating business requirements into visual insights. * Good knowledge of financial instruments, systems and processes related to Back\-Offices (Borrowings and/or Treasury and/or Lending). * Good understanding of payment market standards including the latest ISO 20022 standards. * Experience with payment and reconciliation software such as ION Wallstreet D\&C, SWIFT Alliance Access, Accurate NXG, or similar platforms. * Good presentation and communication skills. * Good coordination skills. * Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office 365\. * Experience in IT projects involving business rules automation and IT algorithm implementation, including understanding conditions, loops, and parsing, would be an advantage. * Experience in project management would be considered an advantage. * Knowledge of a financial back\-office software (ION Wallstreet TRM \- CLM or similar) would be an advantage. * Proficiency in Microsoft Power Platform would be an advantage. * Excellent knowledge of English and/or French (\*\*\*), with a good command of the other. Knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage. Competencies **Find Out More About EIB Core Behavioural Skills Here** To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here *(\*\*\*) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre\-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre\-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of* *level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1\.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.* We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process due to a disability, neurodivergence, or a chronic health condition, please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will manage your request appropriately. By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation. Deadline for applications: **6th May 2026**

title: Business Development Consultant
description: * **Merit\-Based Promotions: Progress as fast as you perform** * **Fast\-Track Growth: Earn up to three promotions in your first year!** * **Lucrative Rewards: Uncapped earnings that grow with your career** If you’ve smashed targets, built relationships, and closed deals in a fast\-paced sales role, recruitment or business development could be your next big move. At Archer, we hire ambitious, commercially minded people who want fast, merit\-based progression, uncapped earnings, and a long\-term, high\-reward career using the skills they already have. Your sales experience isn’t just valued; it’s your launchpad. Step into a role where your drive and results truly pay off. **Why Join the Team?** * **Fast\-Track Your Career** : Move quickly from trainee to leadership and account management roles, with promotions earned as you deliver. * **Level Up Your Skills** : Transform your sales experience into high\-value commercial expertise through tailored training and real\-world experience. * **Get Rewarded as You Rise** : Uncapped commission and bonuses mean your earnings grow alongside your career progression. **What You Will Do:** * Own the full sales lifecycle, from initial outreach to ongoing account development * Create and grow your own client portfolio within fast\-growing industries such as technology, medical devices, pharmaceuticals, and finance * Operate with a high degree of independence while being backed by experienced commercial leadership * Evolve into a strategic commercial partner rather than solely a salesperson **What we’re looking for:** * 3\+ years of commercial experience (1\+ year of sales experience – Inside Sales, B2B, Account Management, Client Onboarding) * Strong communication and confidence on the phone * Drive, ambition, and a genuine interest in building a career **What you’ll get:** * Competitive Base \+ Uncapped Commission (Tiered commission structure – each promotion unlocks greater rewards) \+ Additional Quarterly Bonus * Total Compensation \= Year 1: €47,000\+, Year 2: €70,000\+, Year 3 €100,000\+ * Learning \& Development: Internal programs, mentorship, and the chance to train alongside managers * Clear Progression Path: Up to 13 promotions, including Equity Partner opportunities and Leadership Academy for aspiring managers * Sabbatical: Fully paid sabbatical every 3 years * Industry\-Leading Training: 200\+ modules covering recruitment, business development, leadership, and industry trends * Team Culture: Highly collaborative, supportive environment with team socials and a strong sense of community **Ready to Make the Move?** Contact Ciara O’Flynn at **ciara.oflynn@archer.ie** or **\+353 1 902 6804** to start your journey!

title: Business Development Graduate - Summer 2026
description: **Position: Business Development Graduate** **Locations: Dublin** **Start Date: Summer 2026** **Kickstart your career in sales with a global market leader.** At **KEYENCE** , we’re looking for ambitious graduates ready to take ownership of their careers in a fast\-paced, high\-reward environment. If you thrive on challenges and want to develop world\-class commercial skills, this role is for you. **About the Role** As a **Business Development Graduate** , you’ll be the **CEO of your own territory** , managing client relationships and driving sales. Your week will be split between: * **3 field\-based days** : Visiting clients, delivering product demonstrations, and closing deals. * **2 office\-based days** : Prospecting new business opportunities and building on existing customer relationships alongside your team. You’ll start with an intensive **training programme** combining classroom learning and on\-the\-job mentoring. As you grow, you’ll continue to benefit from structured development in **sales, leadership, and management** —with every manager at KEYENCE promoted from within. **What We Offer** * **Starting Salary:** €37,300 plus €2350 per annum in Location Allowance * **Quarterly Bonus:** Guaranteed minimum bonus year 1 \= €5,700 * **Company Car:** For business and personal use * **Private Healthcare:** Includes dental, optical, audiology, and subsidised gym membership * **Pension Scheme:** Advanced company pension with Zurich * **Contribution towards Private Healthcare** * **Employee Assistance Programme** * **Fully Expensed Travel \& Accommodation** **What We’re Looking For** * A degree in any discipline * Strong motivation and a winning mindset * Must have a full driving licence, valid in Ireland * Eligibility to work in the Ireland by Summer 2026 **About KEYENCE** Founded in 1974, **KEYENCE** is a global leader in automation and control technology, providing cutting\-edge solutions in Sensors, Vision, Measurement, Safety, and Microscopes. Operating in 90% of global industries and generating over **$8 billion** in annual sales, our **Direct Sales model** and focus on innovation have driven industry\-leading profitability and growth.

title: Business Development Representative
description: 📝 **Company Mission** *Our mission is to give the world access to the best products by empowering great businesses to reach their growth potential.* 💼 **About Wayflyer** Today's SMBs need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer. Our unique technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. Since launching in April 2020, we've deployed over $5bn to thousands of businesses worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan. We've become a trusted financing partner for some amazing brands, like True Classic, Little Words Project and Kekoa Foods. Teams at Wayflyer are truly cross\-functional. You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world \- all striving to deliver on a huge opportunity. Check out this video to hear directly from them. 💛 **Culture \& Values at Wayflyer** At Wayflyer, we value being sound people, excellent operators, and ambitious overachievers, working together with integrity, creativity, and bold optimism to deliver exceptional results. To learn more, please visit our website. 🚀 **Your Role at a Glance:** *The key ways you'll bring value to the team* * Reach out to eCommerce founders to introduce Wayflyer and qualify their need for funding to help grow their business * Use creative outreach methods including calls, personalised emails and LinkedIn/social selling to set up meetings for your Account Executive counterpart * Build trust with our customers, acting as a Co\-founder to ensure mutually beneficial relationships * Play a key part in helping us to grow our revenue, predominantly in the US market * You'll join our revenue academy, learning the skills necessary to become one of our top performing Account Executives or Customer Success Associates after 18 months! **🔨 What Makes You a Great Fit** * You're excited by the opportunity to work for Ireland's fastest growing tech company * You have experience working successfully in a target driven sales environment * You enjoy working in a fast\-paced environment \- previous startup experience is a plus (not a necessity). * You have an ownership mentality, always seeking to improve Wayflyer's prospects, even in functions outside of sales. * You're ambitious and eager to build a career in sales * You're fluent in English. 🤝 **What to Expect After You Apply** You can expect to hear from a member of our Talent Acquisition team soon. If we believe you're a good fit, the first step will be a conversation with a recruiter who will guide you through the next stages of the process. The full interview process, which includes meeting the Hiring Manager and completing a case study or interview loop, typically takes approximately two to three weeks. 🏖️ **The Perks of Being at Wayflyer** 🌴 **Time Off That Matters** Recharge with 25 days of paid annual leave, plus public holidays. 🧘 **Your Wellbeing** Access support and resources to support your wellbeing, including our *Wellbeing @ Wayflyer* ERG. 🫀 **Comprehensive Health \& Protection** Stay covered with private healthcare, life insurance, and critical illness cover. 👵 **Secure Your Future with Our Pension Plan** Our pension plan helps you build a strong foundation for tomorrow, starting today. 🍼 **Family\-First Policies** We provide a generous parental and adoptive leave program for both Primary and Secondary caregivers, ensuring you have the dedicated, fully paid time you need to focus on your family. 💰 **Share in Our Success** With our equity scheme, you're not just an employee; you're a stakeholder in our journey. 🌴 **Work From Abroad!** Enjoy the freedom to work remotely from anywhere in the world for up to 60 calendar days a year. 📍Global Offices and Working Policy 🇮🇪 **Dublin, Our Buzzing HQ** Our headquarters operates a hybrid model. The number of in\-office days depends on your team, but the majority of teams and our SLT are typically in 3\-4 days per week, giving you valuable exposure to senior leadership. 🇬🇧 **London, One Big Family** Home to around 30 employees, our brand\-new London office also runs on a hybrid model. You’ll often find our CEO working alongside the team with regular visits. 🇦🇺 **Sydney, Small but Mighty** Our close\-knit Sydney office has around 20 employees and follows a flexible hybrid model, fostering strong collaboration. 🇺🇸 **New York \& Charlotte, Stateside Collaboration** Each of our US offices has 8\-10 employees and operates with a flexible hybrid approach, blending in\-person connection with remote freedom. 🌍 **Remote, Across the US \& Europe** The majority of our Tech org works remotely across the UK and Europe, coming together throughout the year for off\-sites, company kick off and technical kick off. We also have a growing number of remote team members across the US. 💡 **How We Handle Your Personal Data** By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Wayflyer Limited will process your personal data for the purpose of evaluating your suitability for the role. Such processing is based on the need to take steps prior to entering into a potential employment agreement. To learn more about how we handle your personal data, you can contact our privacy team at privacy@wayflyer.com or review our privacy notice at https://wayflyer.com/privacy\-notice.

title: Calling All SDR’s - Still booking meetings, or ready to close?
description: If you’re comfortable where you are, this probably isn’t for you. But if you’re looking for a bigger challenge and a seat at the table, keep reading. We’re hiring for a **Business Developer Account Manager** role, and for a **Recruitment Consultant** role. * **Growth Accelerator Program** – Achieve in 2 years what takes others a decade. * **Proven Promotion Framework** – Join a team where 100% of leadership started in your seat. * **Leadership From Day One** – Be seen, trusted, and developed early. **A Personal Note from the Hiring Team** I’ve sat across from hundreds of candidates who feel "stuck." They have the drive, but they’re in roles where their full potential isn't being explored and they lack true ownership of their career. In my time here, I’ve seen the impossible become the standard. I’ve watched high performers achieve **leadership roles in just 12 months** and seen individuals earn **three promotions in a single year** . Usually, the only thing standing between a stagnant career and meaningful success is the fear of taking the leap. I’ve seen it first hand: those who take the risk are the ones who get rewarded. What are you waiting for? **The Roadmap: What Your First 6 Months Look Like** We don’t leave your growth to chance. We have a tested, step\-by\-step map to get you from contributor to influencer: * **First 30 Days:** You master the system top performers use. You’ll start building pipeline, having real conversations, and hitting early targets with 1:1 support. * **By 60 Days:** Confidence kicks in. You’re running your own pipeline and owning the full sales process—not just booking meetings, but understanding the *why* behind the win. * **By 90 Days:** You’re standing out. Consistent performance brings internal visibility; you are officially recognized as a high\-potential talent. * **By 6 Months:** This is where the shift happens. High performers are already stepping into leadership responsibilities, mentoring others, and gaining real influence across the business. **Why Join Us?** * **Security \& Fairness:** Know that your effort leads somewhere concrete. * **Belonging \& Trust:** We invest in our own people rather than hiring from the outside. * **Purpose:** Experience the reward of having a voice and making a tangible impact on the company’s direction. * **Uncapped Earning Potential:** We offer a competitive base salary \+ **Uncapped Commission with NO LIMIT on your earnings.** Year 1 average total compensation **€45k\+** , and Year 2 **€75k\+** (As you scale into leadership and influence) **What We Look For** * 1–5 years of experience in outbound, B2B sales, inside sales, client relationship, SDR, BDR, or other B2B commercially\-driven environments * A history of success with KPIs, targets, or commission\-based work * Strong communication skills and the drive to grow * EU citizenship or a Stamp 4 visa (we cannot provide sponsorship) **Apply now** through LinkedIn, at cesar.toscano@accesshealthcare.ie, or at \+353 (1\) 572 1161

title: Chargebacks – Processing Specialist
description: **Calling all innovators – find your future at Fiserv.** We’re Fiserv, a global leader in Fintech and payments, and we move money and information in a way that moves the world. We connect financial institutions, corporations, merchants, and consumers to one another millions of times a day – quickly, reliably, and securely. Any time you swipe your credit card, pay through a mobile app, or withdraw money from the bank, we’re involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv. **Job Title** Chargebacks – Processing Specialist**What does a successful Processing Specialist do?** A successful Processing Specialist provides high\-quality operational support on disputes and interchange rejects for a portfolio of clients. They understand Visa and Mastercard rules, end\-to\-end dispute flows, and acquiring processes, and use that knowledge to guide clients on how to respond effectively and within strict deadlines. They proactively monitor scheme file flows, liaise with technical and internal teams to resolve issues, and maintain rigorous compliance standards. With strong analytical skills and attention to detail, they minimize financial losses and operational risk for clients and for Fiserv. As a trusted partner in possibility, they help clients deliver secure, seamless payment experiences that keep pace with the speed of life. **What you will do:** * Support multiple clients on day\-to\-day dispute handling across the full lifecycle, including chargeback, representment, pre\-arbitration, arbitration, and compliance * Advise clients on dispute responses, including evidence requirements, timelines, and best practices to maximize recovery and minimize write\-offs * Analyse interchange and scheme rejects at scale, identify root causes, and recommend corrective actions to clients and internal teams * Monitor incoming and outgoing scheme files (primarily Visa and Mastercard) to ensure completeness, accuracy, and timely processing * Investigate and resolve file and transaction exceptions in collaboration with technical, operations, and other internal teams * Apply scheme and internal compliance rules consistently, maintaining high\-quality documentation and clear audit trails * Produce and maintain reports and dashboards on disputes and interchange rejects, highlighting trends, KPIs, and emerging issues * Prepare clear, compelling PowerPoint materials card payments compliance and regulatory requirements related to disputes and chargebacks * Additional language skills aligned to client needs, and a collaborative mindset for working with technical, title: Chef de Partie
description: Roseville Nursing Home are looking for a Chef de Partie. In this role you will prepare and cook meals for residents, addressing their dietary needs and preferences. You will ensure efficient, safe, and hygienic kitchen operations, collaborating with the team to deliver a positive dining experience. This position involves: Managing special diets, modified meals, and individual preferences. Maintaining kitchen cleanliness and hygiene. Ensuring safe food storage, handling, and preparation. Monitoring stock levels, rotating supplies, and reporting shortages. Keeping records for food hygiene and temperature controls. Contributing to residents' well\-being. **Core Responsibilities:** **1\. Culinary Production \& Specialized Diets** Design nutritious meal plans for residents. Prepare special and texture\-modified diets (Levels 4, 5, 6\). Enrich meals as directed by a Dietician or SLT. **2\. Supervision \& Leadership** Train junior staff in food preparation and hygiene. Ensure timely meal service and coordinate with the nursing staff. **3\. Food Safety \& Compliance (HACCP)** Documentation: Maintain records of temperatures and cleaning schedules. Allergen Management: Manage allergens and prevent cross\-contamination. Audits: Prepare the kitchen for EHO and HIQA inspections. **Candidate Profile:** **We are seeking an individual with:** A minimum of 2 years' professional kitchen experience is required (healthcare experience beneficial). Qualifications: A Professional Cookery qualification (FETAC Level 5/6 or equivalent). A Primary Food Hygiene / HACCP Level 2 certificate. Available to work varied shift patterns days including weekends, across Monday to Sunday. Salary starting: €36,605\.00 per annum, based on 39 hours per week. Location: Roseville Nursing Home, 49 Meath Road, Bray, Co. Wicklow, A98XW24 Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €36,605\.00 per year Ability to commute/relocate: * Bray, County Wicklow: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Experience: * Kitchen management: 2 years (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Chef de partie
description: Kingscourt Castle ltd T/A Cabra castle , kingscourt, Co. Cavan A82 EC64 is recruiting for Chef de partie to JOIN OUR TEAM Candidates must have minimum 3 years relevant experience Full time position 39 hours per week As a **Chef de Partie**, you will be responsible for supporting the Head Chef and Sous Chef in a busy hotel kitchen. Your primary duty is to ensure the consistent delivery of high\-quality food, meeting and exceeding guest expectations in terms of food quality and presentation. You will also be responsible for handling purchase orders and ensuring that any unauthorized items are not received. **Main Duties:** * **Food Preparation \& Daily Operations**: * Take care of daily food preparation and tasks assigned by senior chefs to meet the standards set by the hotel. * Follow instructions and recommendations from the Head Chef, Sous Chef, and other superiors to complete daily tasks efficiently. * Estimate the daily production needs and check the quality of raw and cooked food products to ensure high standards are met. * Ensure the production, preparation, and presentation of food are of the highest quality at all times. * **Team Coordination \& Supervision**: * Supervise junior chefs or commis to ensure a smooth operation in the kitchen. * Coordinate daily tasks with the Sous Chef to ensure an efficient workflow. * Guide and train junior staff to ensure they are motivated and working in an economically efficient manner. * **Menu \& Recipe Knowledge**: * Have full knowledge of all menu items, including their recipes, methods of production, and presentation standards. * Assist the Head Chef and Sous Chef in the creation of menus and new recipes. * Stay updated on new products, preparation techniques, and industry trends to ensure the kitchen remains innovative and competitive. * **Quality Control \& Guest Satisfaction**: * Ensure the highest levels of guest satisfaction by maintaining food quality and managing operating and food costs. * Assess the quality control of dishes and ensure that they meet hotel standards. * Check the proper storage of food items and periodically check expiry dates to ensure food safety. * **Health, Safety \& Hygiene**: * Ensure compliance with all safety procedures, including fire drills, and the correct use of equipment and utensils. * Ensure adherence to hygiene and sanitation standards in line with HACCP regulations and relevant food safety legislation. * Be responsible for personal hygiene and cleanliness, setting an example for others on and off duty. * Maintain good preservation standards for the handling of food products, including correct temperature storage. * **Equipment Maintenance \& Communication**: * Operate and maintain all department equipment, reporting any defects promptly. * Ensure effective communication between kitchen staff and maintain a secure, friendly working environment. * Establish and maintain effective inter\-departmental relationships for smooth operations. * **Reporting \& Feedback**: * Provide daily feedback to the Sous Chef and Head Chef on production, quality, and any issues arising during the day. * Collect and report issues, including any incidents or accidents, and take appropriate action. * Attend all necessary training courses as instructed by management. * **Additional Responsibilities**: * Assist with functions, events, and last\-minute changes to the menu as per the daily requirements discussed with the Sous Chef and Executive Chef. * Support the wider team in banquet and room service operations when necessary. * Ensure all safety and emergency procedures are adhered to, ensuring the health and safety of guests and staff. **Key Responsibilities:** * Take ownership of your section in the kitchen, ensuring quality and presentation meet the hotel's high standards. * Supervise and train junior kitchen staff to ensure a productive and positive working environment. * Stay aware of all relevant food preparation, storage, and sanitation procedures. * Provide excellent service in terms of food quality, meeting deadlines, and maintaining cleanliness and safety. * Communicate effectively with other kitchen and restaurant staff to ensure a seamless dining experience for guests.

title: Chief Executive Officer
description: **The Opportunity – Chief Executive Officer** Reporting to the Board, this is an exciting opportunity for a Senior Leader to build on the great work already completed and lead DCM through its next phase of growth. While you will be accountable for our strategic growth and governance, you will also thrive on being close to the action, leading by example and getting involved when needed, to support the team with operational issues. Please see below an outline of the key objectives and responsibilities of the role: ***Leadership*** – Lead and inspire the management team in a supportive way to deliver our vision. ***Quality*** – Deliver quality services through oversight of all regulatory compliance. ***Development*** – Influence and drive the strategic plan for DCM working to achieve the goals set out for the organisation to continue to thrive as an Older Persons Charity. ***Finance*** – Oversee annual budgets and closely monitor financial performance. **Responsibilities** **People Management** * Support an open, supportive and friendly culture across the organisation delivering on the company’s core values, vision and mission. * Ensure regular touch points with the Senior Management Team and that each role is filled with the skillset required to operate effectively. * Oversee resource management to ensure that sufficient staffing levels are maintained. **Business Development** * Build and develop relationships with the HSE and other partners. * Build brand awareness among potential new employees, residents and families through regular engagement at events and via social media. * Maintain consistency across the brand and messaging. * Deliver on agreed growth targets and strategies. **Quality Assurance** * Ensure there is effective quality assurance across all services. * Oversee and assist with the completion of a suite of reports for each subcommittee of the board. * Oversee and regularly review service level policies and procedures. * Monitor IT systems and regularly review how to improve the automation of practices to make service provision more efficient. * Ensure complaints are dealt within the policy timeframe and monitor trends. * Ensure the organisation has a robust data protection policy and procedure to meet the requirements of GDPR. **Finance** * Responsibility for budget delivery and financial performance working closing with the Finance Director and management team. * Annual (or more frequent) contribution to budget preparation and forecasting. * With the support of HR, maintain an oversight of internal pay policies and market developments/ trends. * Continually look at opportunity to bring in new income and enhance current profit margin to ensure DCM is financially viable and sustainable. **Operations / Leadership** * Be hands on as required to act as a support for the managers and staff teams across all services. * Have overall responsibility for the delivery of services to our residents / clients. * Be a visible leader that engages well with staff and service users. **Other** * Some on\-call responsibilities will be required, commensurate with a role of this level. **About You** We’re looking for a proven leader who is passionate about leading a high quality, people centred organisation. Someone who recognises that as CEO, you will provide both strategic direction and visible daily leadership across all teams to ensure we maintain our excellent standards of care. The successful candidate will be someone who finds energy in being close to the heart of the work, values strong relationships and demonstrates hands\-on leadership in services, ensuring decisions are grounded in the realities of care delivery while embracing DCM’s values and ethos. **You’ll bring:** * At least five years’ senior management experience, ideally within healthcare, social care, housing, older persons services or a related service sector. * Strong financial experience particularly in preparing and managing budgets. * Strong commercial acumen, with demonstrated experience in developing organisations to meet market opportunities. * Excellent relationship building and communication skills, with the ability to represent DCM to a range of stakeholders internal and external. * A collaborative, people focused leadership style that motivates and inspires others. * A focus on quality and governance. * A relevant qualification in management, nursing, health and social care (or related field) is desirable. **What you can expect from us:** **Summary Terms \& Conditions** * Accountable to the Board of Trustees and line managed by the Chair of Trustees. * Based between sites and the head office in Mount Tabor, Sandymount Green, with provision also for home working. * Able to work a full time 39 hours per week with flexibly as required including some evening meetings. * Able to travel between sites as required – there are no expenses paid for this travel as they are in close proximity. * Salary in the range €105\-€120K depending on experience * Eligible for 28 days annual leave plus statutory holidays. * Eligible for membership of the defined contribution pension scheme including a matching employer’s contribution up to 8%. * Eligibility for performance related bonus linked to Company performance and KPI’s. **The Process** To express your interest in this role, please send your CV and short covering letter to our Head of People Radek Warchola at: radek.warchola@dublincentralmission.ie Following review of applicants the interview process will be communicated to those shortlisted. Closing date for application is Friday 15th May 2026\.

title: Civil Defense Tech Co-Founder / CMO (100 % remote) (m/f/d)
description: We are looking to hire ambitious entrepreneurs to start and scale their own startups. We are serial entrepreneurs, for example Paul Müller (founder Adjust, €1\.2B exit) and Petter Made (founder SumUp, €8B) who are eager to support outlier personalities and serial entrepreneurs to build €1B\+ companies. Our offer: * A salary while you build your startup as you will directly be employed by us. Alternatively, you can opt for up to **€500k** in funding. * **1:1 sparring with unicorn founders** on a weekly basis * **Community**: Access to the top 0\.1% of founders, peers and investors * **Team building**: Hiring top notch talent supported through our network (over 50,000 professionals) * **Distribution**: Support in reaching product\-market\-fit and building up a sales force / marketing machine * **Funding support** for securing a multi\-million euro funding round within 12 months (on average, EWOR Fellows raise \> €2M after our Grand Pitch) One of our fellows set a record for Europe’s largest pre\-seed round by a first\-time founder, securing a €12M pre\-seed investment. **Tasks** --------- * You will own, build, and run your startup in fields such as Civil Defense Tech * You will embark on an extensive personal development journey crafted by unicorn founders and follow a fully customised programme enhancing your goal, time, and energy management * You will receive support in hiring through our network to over 50,000 professionals and advice as well as best practices from serial entrepreneurs * You will receive intensive coaching to make your startup ready to raise millions in funding * You will iterate your product with us until having reached product\-market\-fit and receive support in building up a sales force or creating a marketing engine respectively **Requirements** ---------------- * You are based in Europe or the Americas or open to relocate * You are willing to take full responsibility for your own startup and scale it to €100M\+ in revenues * You have excellent communication skills in the English language Join us and build a €1B\+ company with us!

title: Cleaner

title: Client Strategist (Arabic, Russian or EU Language)
description: **Summary** At Apple, we believe in the power of technology to enrich people’s lives. Everything we build is designed—first and foremost—with helping people in mind. We believe advertising is no different, and that its value should come from how it benefits customers, not what it extracts from them. Our Ad Platforms team applies that belief by delivering ads that add value, helping people discover content, supporting creators, and protecting and respecting everyone’s privacy. Our technology powers ads in the App Store, Apple News, Stocks, and Apple TV. We help developers and marketers of all sizes drive discovery of their apps across the App Store. Our display ads in Apple News and Stocks aid advertisers in promoting their products alongside trusted content in a brand\-safe environment, while supporting publishers and journalists. We help advertisers connect with captivated audiences with our sponsorship integrations and experiences in live sports on Apple TV. Everything we do is with the unwavering commitment to privacy you expect from Apple. Because when advertising is done right, it benefits everyone. **Description** In this role, you have a unique opportunity to build and shape Apple’s Growth Accounts Ads team. The segment of clients are exciting and requires a best\-in\-class partnership to achieve their business aspirations. With your effort and contribution this will become one of the highest performing and fun Growth Accounts Sales teams around! You should have a real passion for helping small and medium businesses succeed, have very strong communication and interpersonal skills, be highly analytical, resourceful, client\-focused, and team\-oriented, and can work independently to meet revenue and business objectives. You have a proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets in the ads space with performance\-type advertisers and agencies. Ideally, we are looking or Russian and/or Arabic Speakers, and individuals who speak fluent EU languages. **Responsibilities** * Build a strong book of business and maintaining trusted relationships with advertisers and app developer clients. * Maintain a robust revenue pipeline through email, phone and in\-person outreach and moving advertising deals through planned, pitched, agreed, and implemented phases. * Develop comprehensive performance marketing strategies based on market research, data analysis and client objectives. * Identify new opportunities for clients to grow their business through Apple Ads. * Demonstrate product and industry expertise in client interactions, educating clients on the value of advertising strategies of Apple Ads. **Minimum Qualifications** * Proven demonstrable experience in performance media sales, including experience working with a self\-serve platform is required. * A proven track record of overachievement and meeting or exceeding sales targets in the digital ads space. * A deep understanding of performance advertiser market strategies and their success measurement tactics. * Exceptional communication and presentation skills, capable of delivering impactful messages both in person and over the phone. * An innate desire to make a difference, driven by self\-motivation and a commitment to excel. * Ability to develop initial campaign success across a portfolio of clients, which then leads to credibility and trust, then comes with longer\-term success. * Strong analytical skills, with the ability to leverage internal and external data sets to facilitate business growth for your clients. * Fluent in Russian, Arabic and/or another EU language **Preferred Qualifications** * Natural collaborator with a team\-oriented mindset, capable of thriving in a dynamic and exciting environment. * Demonstrated professionalism and a positive demeanor, with a desire to develop business acumen and client\-centricity. * Proficiency in active listening and asking insightful questions for continuous learning.

title: Cloud Security Engineer

Opportunity Summary In this new position, they are seeking an experienced Cloud Security Engineer. This role offers exciting opportunities for growth and impact as this organisation scales their business and continues to innovate. As a Cloud Security Engineer, you will: * Design, implement, and maintain security controls across cloud environments * Secure cloud infrastructure, platforms, and services that support internally developed and externally facing applications * Partner closely with Infrastructure, DevOps, Engineering, and business teams * Ensure cloud security controls align with industry best practices and regulatory requirements * Proactively identify emerging risks and evaluate new technologies * Contribute to shaping their long\-term cloud security strategy Role \& Responsibilities: * Design and implement secure cloud architectures across public cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP * Develop and enforce cloud security standards, guardrails, and policies using infrastructure as code and policy as code frameworks * Monitor cloud environments for misconfigurations, vulnerabilities, and active threats * Implement and manage identity and access management controls, including least privilege access and role\-based access control * Partner with DevOps and Engineering teams to integrate security into CI/CD pipelines and cloud native workflows * Deploy and manage cloud security tools such as CSPM, CWPP, container security platforms, and cloud workload monitoring solutions * Conduct cloud security assessments, architecture reviews, and risk evaluations * Support incident response activities related to cloud environments, including investigation and remediation * Ensure compliance with relevant regulatory requirements and industry frameworks * Maintain awareness of evolving cloud threats and emerging security technologies Skills \& Experience * Come from an employment background in enterprise\-scale, global, complex, public cloud security * Have a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Cybersecurity, or a related field * Proven experience as a Cloud Security Engineer or in a similar, cloud\-focused security role * Extensive hands\-on experience with major cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP * Deep understanding of cloud networking, architecture principles, and shared responsibility models * Experience with infrastructure as code tools such as Terraform or CloudFormation * Familiarity with containerisation and orchestration technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes * Experience implementing cloud security controls, including encryption, logging, monitoring, and identity management * Knowledge of regulatory requirements and frameworks such as NIST, ISO 27001, CIS benchmarks, and SOC 2 * Experience with DevSecOps practices and integrating security into automated deployment pipelines * Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills in complex cloud environments * Comfortable working collaboratively as part of a global team * Ability to work under pressure, meet deadlines, and solve complex problems with initiative and drive 2 Ability to communicate effectively in both business and technical settings * Strategic thinker with an innovative mindset and strong attention to detail You must be eligible to live and work in Ireland with no restrictions, as sponsorship now or in the future is not available.

title: Cloud Solutions Architect (Daily Rate)

The Cloud Architect will support a large\-scale digital and cloud transformation programme. This is a daily rate contract role focused on designing, delivering, and optimising secure, scalable cloud infrastructure solutions across AWS and/or Azure environments. You will operate within a Cloud Centre of Excellence style function, contributing hands\-on architecture expertise, establishing best practice patterns, and accelerating cloud adoption across enterprise platforms. **Key Responsibilities** * Design and deliver secure, scalable, and cost\-efficient cloud architecture solutions aligned to enterprise and regulatory requirements * Refine and translate business requirements into robust technical designs * Provide strategic guidance on cloud adoption, platform optimisation, and architectural best practice * Drive innovation and cost optimisation across public cloud environments * Present architectural solutions to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Ensure security\-by\-design principles are embedded across all solutions * Develop reusable, repeatable cloud patterns to accelerate delivery and reduce platform overhead * Collaborate with engineering teams to implement, deploy, and enhance cloud capabilities * title: Co Managing Partner

In parallel, the company has also launched RoleSense — a Recruitment SaaS ( product by ALLY EXECUTIVE HR) and workforce intelligence platform focused on: www.rolesense.in/eu * Contextual Resume Evaluation * Skill \& competency\-based fitment analysis * Vendor Marketplace Place * OnContract workforce supply model ( Focus on Europe) * OnContract Bench management for staffing agencies * Unified hiring workflow for corporates and agencies The current objective is to onboard a strategic Managing Partner / Investment Partner based in Europe. We are open to engaging with experienced professionals and business leaders across European markets who can contribute towards scaling our regional operations and expanding the business across UAE, Europe, Dublin ( Ireland) and the broader EMEA region. The ideal partner would play a key role in: • Investing in business growth and operational expansion • Building and scaling European operations • Developing enterprise, staffing, and workforce partnerships • Establishing local recruitment, sales, and business development teams • Contributing to the growth and positioning of the RoleSense platform • Supporting long\-term regional expansion and strategic market penetration • Creating cross\-border business opportunities between Europe, UAE, and India We are looking for a long\-term business\-building partner who can combine strategic vision, market relationships, and operational execution to help build a scalable workforce and recruitment technology ecosystem. **Ideal Profile** * Proven experience as an investor in recruitment business venture or business leader * Strong network across enterprise organizations, HR tech, staffing, or SaaS ecosystems * Experience in scaling startups or growth\-stage companies * Understanding of talent acquisition, workforce supply, or platform businesses (preferred) * Ability to contribute beyond capital—through strategy, connections, and mentorship **What We Are Building** * A unified platform (RoleSense) connecting corporates, staffing agencies, and contract talent * AI\-driven candidate evaluation and role matching * Vendor marketplace for full\-time and contract hiring * Workforce supply model for global enterprises **Why Join Us** * Opportunity to be part of a high\-growth, scalable business model * Strong domain expertise across high\-demand industries * Early\-stage entry into a platform\-driven talent ecosystem * Dual\-market presence (India \& UAE) with global expansion roadmap **Engagement Model** * Equity partnership / strategic investment * Flexible engagement structure based on mutual alignment

Grangegorman * National College of Ireland * Stakeholders in the local community, including parents, children and practitioners * National Community Families lead agency staff * Community Families Senior Specialist Key Responsibilities **Programme Development and Delivery:** * To recruit, hire, train and induct new Home Visiting Practitioners * Supervising and supporting Home Visiting Practitioners * Mentoring and coaching new and existing Home Visiting Practitioners. * To plan and deliver ongoing Home Visitor training. * Undertake home visits for own programme families (caseload of 1\-2 families). * To order stock and organise deliveries of books and toys. * To recruit, interview and select families to participate in the programme. **Administration and Reporting:** * Prepare and maintain records, reports and/or test data on the programme and participating children. * Participate in research activities. * To assist with the management of the Community Families budget and accounts. **Community Engagement, Partnerships and Networking:** * Participate in local networks, interagency meetings and community events and liaise with a range of stakeholders including PHN, Tusla, Maternity Hospitals, PCHP, CES, Pobal and other statutory and voluntary bodies around the development of the Community Families Programme and opportunities for shared learning and collaboration. * Use an interagency approach to address challenges faced by families, attend and lead Meitheals for programme families in addition to attending case conferences where required * Organise and implement events for the programme families in conjunction with other Home Visiting and Parent Support team members. * To organise, plan and post on ELI’s social media platforms. * Any other duties as assigned by the Grangegorman Senior Specialist, Community Families Senior Specialist, ELI Director or Assistant Director. Skills and Experience required: * Relevant 3rd level qualification e.g. Early Years Education, Education, Social Care, Psychology etc. * Minimum of 2 years’ experience of working with children, young people, families and statutory agencies. * Experience supervising staff, facilitating meetings and delivering training. * Excellent communication skills including active listening, modelling and non\-verbal cues. * Reflective practitioner. * Ability to work with marginalised families and have an understanding of the issues of marginalisation * Excellent administrative and organisational skills * Excellent IT skills in Ms Excel, Ms Word, CRM systems and other relevant research packages etc. * Professional interest in early years education and family\-based learning. * Ability to deal effectively with a wide variety of college personnel, statutory, community and corporate stakeholders and other outside individuals/organisations * Flexible approach to work as evening and weekend work may be required * Ability to operate as a team player in a cross disciplinary, integrated team, sharing roles and responsibilities * An energetic, confident and pro\-active individual with strong interpersonal and communication skills * Good time management skills with the ability to produce results to deadlines. **Contract:** 12 Months Specific Purpose **Salary:** €31,059 \- €43,485 (Administrator Salary Band) Pro Rata **Annual Leave:** 24 Days per annum pro rata **Hours:** 33 hours per week Application Process: To apply, please visit NCI Recruitment end dates for applications are 19th of May 2026 With subject to change Interviews are expected to be held between 8th\-12th June 2026 **Note:** The functions and responsibilities assigned to the Community Families Coordinator are based on the current stated role and objectives of the College. These functions and responsibilities may alter in line with any change in the role and objectives of the College. The successful applicant will be subject to Garda Vetting and must not have any convictions under the Sex Offenders Act 2001\. The Importance of Confidentiality We would like to assure you that protecting confidentiality is our number one priority. You can expect that all enquires, applications and all aspects of the proceedings are treated as strictly confidential and are not disclosed to anyone, outside those directly involved in that aspect of the process. **Candidates' Obligations** In submitting a completed Curriculum Vitae and supporting documentation, candidates are declaring: * That all the particulars furnished in their application are true and correct without omission and that they are aware of the qualifications and particulars for this position. * That to the best of their knowledge that there is nothing in relation to their conduct, character or personal background of any nature that would adversely affect the position of trust in which they would be placed by virtue of this appointment. * Their irrevocable consent to NCI making such enquiries as deemed necessary in respect of their suitability for the post in respect of which their application is made. * Their acceptance and confirmation of the entitlement of NCI, as appropriate to, reject their application, or to terminate their employment (in the event of a contract of employment having been entered into on foot of this competition) if they have omitted to furnish NCI with any information relevant to their application or their continued employment or where they have made any false statement or misrepresentation relevant to this application or their continuing employment with the College. \# Candidates should note that they may be required to submit documentary evidence in support of any particulars given by them in their application for the post. **Data Protection Acts 1988 to 2018 and the Freedom of Information Acts 1997 to 2014 as amended** Data collected for the purposes of recruitment activities *NCI conducts recruitment processes to fill vacancies within the college. When applying for these competitions applicants are asked to submit a range of documents, e.g., a completed application form, CV and/or a personal statement or cover letter. For the purposes of recruitment activities, we will not collect any personal data that we do not need to assess your candidature for a role with us.* **Legal Basis for Processing** *NCI ’s legal basis for the processing of this data is a combination of individual consent, contractual necessity and legal obligations.* **Withdrawal of Consent** *Applicants can withdraw their consent for the processing of their personal data at any time by notifying the College. It is important to note that withdrawal of consent prior to the completion of the process will be considered as a withdrawal of the application.* What we do with your data *People who are directly employed by NCI and are based in Ireland process all the personal data collected for both recruitment and HR purposes.* **Shortlisting** *HR draft up the shortlisting documents and provide the initial screening to determine if a person meets the essential criteria of the competition. A shortlisting panel is generally made up of two senior grade staff members from the relevant department for which the role is being recruited.* **Sharing of your data** *Where you have been successfully shortlisted for a post, each interview panel member will be furnished with a copy of the relevant information of those who will be attending interview in order that they can review applications in advance of the interview process. All such information will be returned to NCI and shredded following the competition process. Some of the interview panel will be external interviewers, who are not employees of NCI but are engaged for the purpose and are strictly subject to the rules and policies of the College.* **Access** *Applicants can request and receive access to their data at any time and can request and receive a copy of this data, in electronic/transferable format.* **Erasure** *Applicants can request the data held be erased. In this case, the application for a position is considered withdrawn.* **Rectification** *Applicants can have any incorrect information corrected.* **Objection / Withdraw** *Applications can object to this information being processed and/or can request to withdraw from the application process* **Complain** *Applicants can make a complaint to our internal* *Information Governance and Data Protection Officer* **NCI** **Information Governance and Data Protection Officer** National College of Ireland, Mayor Street, IFSC, Dublin 1 Tel (Direct): \+ 353 1 4498 523 ; Tel (Reception): \+ 353 1 4498 500 And/or make a complaint to the relevant authority Data Protection Commission, 21 Fitzwilliam Square South, Dublin 2, D02 RD28 Tel: \+353 (0\) 761 104 800 National College of Ireland is an equal opportunities employer and is a Member of the Athena SWAN Charter. NCI is committed to serving our diverse community and welcomes applications from underrepresented groups.

title: Company Secretarial Trainee
description: **Job Description** **Company Secretarial Trainee** This is an excellent opportunity for an ambitious and driven candidate who is keen to progress within Ireland’s fastest growing professional services firm. We are looking to hire a Company Secretarial Trainee to join our team. We are looking for graduates/postgraduates who are ambitious and want to start their career in the area of company secretarial/governance. **Responsibilities** Reporting to the company secretarial management team, you will perform the usual duties associated with a trainee role: * Preparation and filing of statutory forms and attendance at the Companies Registration Office * Arranging for company formations and branch registrations * Updating the company secretarial database * Maintenance of company secretarial records to include updating Minute Books and Statutory Register * Dealing with queries and requests, on a day to day basis, from clients, auditors, solicitors and internal staff, including company searches * Taking ownership of a portfolio of clients and being responsible for ensuring that clients are meeting their statutory obligations * Keeping up to date with filings, and producing documentation in respect of the Annual General Meeting and other minutes, as required * Responsibility for billing clients and debtors’ collection * Filing, archiving and general administrative duties **Key Requirements** * Have a desire to complete corporate governance or company secretarial qualification * Experience in an office environment preferable however not essential * Excellent MS Office skills * Highly organised * Excellent attention to detail * An ability to prioritise and multi\-task is essential * Familiar with statutory obligations under the Irish Companies Act is an advantage * Computer literate with experience of word, excel and company secretarial package eg. GEMS, Blueprint etc is an advantage * Strong client focus with excellent oral and written communication skills **About Us** **We are Grant Thornton** Grant Thornton Ireland is rapidly approaching 3,000 people, in 9 offices across Ireland, Isle of Man, Gibraltar and Bermuda. With a presence in over 149 countries around the world and a global network of 73,000 people, we bring our clients the local knowledge, national expertise and global presence to help them succeed – wherever they’re located. At GT, we work as ***trusted advisors*** , bringing local knowledge and national expertise, with a global presence, to help businesses succeed – wherever they are located. We make business more personal by investing in building relationships and empowering our clients to make the right decisions for their organisation now and for the future. Whether that is working with the public sector to build thriving communities, with regulators and financial institutions to build trust, or with a diverse range of businesses to help them achieve their goals, Grant Thornton Ireland work hard to support clients to act on the issues that matter. **At GT Ireland we don’t just predict your future, we build it** . **A Career at GT** Looking for a more fulfilling role in professional services? One where fresh thinking, collaboration and diversity are valued? At Grant Thornton we do things differently. **What does this mean for you?** A career in a more inclusive working environment, a more collaborative work culture, a more supported, flexible working role, more possibilities to grow and more opportunities to help shape the future for your clients. We respect and value your experience. And we want you to bring your authentic self to work and be at your best. It is how it should be. **Grow with us** At Grant Thornton, we care about our people and work hard to make you feel valued. If you are looking to deepen and develop your skills, knowledge, and experience throughout your career, then that is what you will get, and more. **Our Benefits** Please follow this link for information on our generous benefits package. **About The Team** **Equity, diversity and inclusion** At Grant Thornton, we provide equitable opportunities for all our colleagues. We are a responsible, sustainable business where equity, diversity and inclusion (ED\&I) is at the forefront of our workplace culture agenda, and today, we continue to build and develop on our existing ED\&I structure and strategy to meet our workplace culture needs. People are at the heart of our business and teams built with varied individuals present diverse viewpoints, which need to be heard and valued. We are all at our best when we are able to be ourselves and we view integrity and authenticity as integral values to bring to our day\-to\-day work\-life at the firm. We are excited to see the personality and perspectives you will bring to our team because we know we will all benefit from them. Diversity of thought, background and experience enables better decision\-making, improves the quality of our delivery, and helps us to meet the needs of our clients. Our firm is built on people and their ideas, so we want to hear all the new perspectives and fresh thinking you have to offer. You form the bedrock of our firm’s best\-practice principles and we will champion you as leaders from day one. **Reward And Benefits** Our reward and benefits are designed to create an environment where our people can flourish. We are committed to building a culture where our people have access to the necessary benefits to help promote a healthy lifestyle and thrive. **Recognition** We want to create a culture of recognition and celebrating success, by saying thank you to people who surpass our expectations and recognising the right values and behaviours. Our Shout Out recognition scheme is our way of highlighting and promoting achievements. Whether you simply want to say thank you, celebrate a special occasion or give an award for doing something exceptional, you can do all of this and more through the scheme.

title: Computer Aided Design Technician
description: We are seeking an experienced CAD Technician to support the delivery of civil engineering projects, with a focus on active travel and urban infrastructure schemes. You will play a key role in the production and coordination of high\-quality drawings, working closely with Engineers and multidisciplinary teams. **About the Company** Clifton Scannell Emerson Associates (CSEA) is a long\-established Civil, Structural and Transportation Engineering consultancy with over 70 years’ experience delivering high\-quality design and project management services across Ireland and internationally. We are trusted on major infrastructure and building projects, combining technical excellence with practical, buildable solutions. As an Engineers Ireland CPD Accredited Employer, we are committed to continuous professional development, mentoring, and career progression within a collaborative, team\-focused environment where people are supported to grow and succeed. **Key Responsibilities:** * Preparation of civil engineering drawings, including plans, sections, and details, across all project stages * Development and management of drawing packages in accordance with project requirements and standards * Coordination of drawings with other disciplines, including structures, drainage, and landscape design * Assisting Engineers in the preparation of design documentation and technical outputs * Ensuring drawings comply with relevant standards, specifications, and company procedures * Updating drawings based on design changes and project feedback * Supporting BIM processes and ensuring alignment with project BIM requirements * Maintaining drawing registers and document control procedures **Competencies** * Minimum 7\+ years’ experience in civil engineering drafting, ideally within active travel or public realm projects * Strong proficiency in AutoCAD * Experience producing drawings for all project stages (feasibility through to construction) * Experience working within multidisciplinary project teams * Good understanding of civil engineering principles and design workflows * Familiarity with Irish design standards and guidance * Awareness of BIM processes and requirements * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Good communication and collaboration abilities * Experience with Civil 3D is advantageous **Benefits** * Competitive salary * Hybrid and flexible working arrangements * Pension scheme * Ongoing CPD and professional development support * Mentoring and structured training programmes * Regular company events and social activities * Opportunity to work on high\-profile infrastructure and building projects

title: Corporate Governance Graduate Programme 2026
description: ### **Description** BDO Ireland is seeking a skilled Trainee to join our successful and expanding Corporate Governance team. This is a 3 year training contract based in our Dublin firm. BDO Ireland has an ambitious strategy which envisages significant future business growth across existing and new services. This growth will be underpinned by a people strategy which will establish BDO as a home for top talent and create an environment where our people can develop and grow. We are looking for an ambitious, forward thinking high\-achiever seeking a new challenge in a progressive and forward\-thinking firm. In addition to a competitive package, this position offers opportunities for progression, challenging projects, and learning and development in a collaborative environment. At BDO you can be yourself \- we value you as an individual and want you to realise your aspirations. You have a voice and your opinion counts. We give you the challenge and support you need, and you can shape your own career according to your strengths, skills and personality. As part of the fifth largest global professional services firm, BDO is one of Ireland’s leading accountancy, tax, advisory and consulting organisations. Working at BDO enhances your professional development at all stages of your career. Few firms offer the same breadth and quality of work, responsibility and experience. We recognise and reward your high performance and you have the opportunity to excel, progress and share in our success. We are clear on what we expect from you and what you can expect in return. The successful candidate will work as part of a small team within the department, with a varied workload including responsibility for a large portfolio of Irish, UK clients across many industry sectors. ### **Key Responsibilities** * Preparing and filing Annual Returns * Drafting Minutes for Board and Shareholder Meetings * Statutory post incorporation changes to include, but not limited to, appointment and resignation of officers, transferring and allotting shares and changes to the Constitution * Incorporating new Companies * Striking\-off dormant Companies * Preparing and filing statutory forms with the Companies Registration Office in Dublin, Companies House in the UK and the Isle of Man Company Registry * Maintaining Statutory Registers and Minute Books * Developing and managing relationships with internal and external clients as necessary * Assisting where required on ad\-hoc projects ### **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise** ***Essential**** Bachelor’s degree (or expected degree) in Law, Business or a related discipline with a minimum of a 2:1 or above * Strong organisational and time management skills * High attention to detail and accuracy * Strong written and verbal communication skills * Professional, discreet, and able to handle confidential information * Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) ***Desirable**** Interest in corporate governance, company secretarial work, or compliance * Exposure to governance, legal, or regulatory environments (e.g. internship, placement, student society role) * Understanding of basic company law principles * Willingness to pursue a professional governance qualification (e.g. CGI/ICSA) ***Personal Attributes**** Proactive and eager to learn * Reliable and well\-organised * Strong ethical judgement * Comfortable working with senior stakeholders * Team\-oriented with the ability to work independently ### **Benefits** *Office Facilities** State of the art high tech office in a Central Dublin City Centre location * Onsite gym, with changing \& shower facilities * Secure bike parking * Onsite café with complimentary barista *Health Benefits \&* *Leave Entitlements* * Together Anywhere Leave * Annual Leave Purchase Scheme * Paid Study Leave * Life Leave * Adoptive, Paternity, Fertility \& Maternity Leave * Take a Me Day * Digital Doctor access * Mini Health Assessments * Expanded Health Insurance providers *Work Equipment \& Resources** Laptop * Annual Professional Subscription * Career Progression * L\&D initiatives *Income Benefits** Life Assurance * Green Travel Allowance * Exam Prizes * Start Up Allowance * Pension Contribution ### **About BDO Ireland** As part of the fifth largest global professional services firm, BDO stands as a leading force in Ireland's accountancy, tax, advisory, and consulting realm. Working with us fuels your career's purpose at every turn, offering unmatched breadth, quality, responsibility, and AI exposure. BDO Ireland is a key player in the international BDO network—the world's largest European\-led accounting group. With an excellent partner\-to\-staff ratio, our 91,054 experts collaborate with clients, delivering impactful advice from 1,658 locations across 167 countries. At BDO, we prioritise how we treat our people—colleagues and clients alike. Our international recognition for a mindful and compassionate company culture reflects our commitment to placing individuals at the heart of all we pursue.
