title: MES Design Engineer
description: **Why SimoTech** What is it like to work at SimoTech? With a strong collaborative teamwork culture based on respect, trust and excellence, we play a critical partnering role to our clients’ life\-changing supply of products to patients. In return, there is excellent salary, benefits, career progression, educational support and much more. **MES Design Engineer 1568** SimoTech has a new opportunity for an MES Design Engineer to join our team, supporting a leading pharmaceutical client in Dublin. This position offers hybrid or remote working options. This role is focused on the design and configuration of Korber (Werum) PAS\-X MBRs primarily within the Form/Fill and Packaging areas. The successful candidate will support both commercial manufacturing and new product introductions, working across the full MBR lifecycle from requirements gathering to testing and deployment. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, configure, and test Korber (Werum) PAS\-X MBRs from initial concept through to final approval. * Collaborate cross\-functionally with manufacturing, quality, automation, and IT teams to gather and analyse user requirements. * Manage the full MBR development lifecycle, including design, configuration, testing, deployment, and documentation using HP Application Lifecycle Management. * Provide support and technical expertise for the ongoing development of the site MES system, development of MBRs, and towards improving MES business processes. * Take part in the training of EBR end\-users and the ongoing development, review, and updating of all relevant SOPs. * Provide technical expertise for equipment integration design, configuration, and testing to allow MBR interaction with shop floor equipment. * Carry out troubleshooting and fault\-finding for MES and all relevant interfaces as required. **Key Requirements** * Bachelors degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Life Sciences, or related field. * 3\+ years’ MES design experience in a pharmaceutical environment. * Hands\-on experience with Koerber (Werum) PAS\-X, with strong proficiency in MBR design. * Working knowledge of MES integration with Level 2 automation systems such as SCADA, DeltaV, and PI. * Excellent communication and problem\-solving skills with experience working closely with end users and key business stakeholders. * Experience working in syringe/vial manufacturing operations is preferred. **What SimoTech Can Offer** * Role provides a high degree of autonomy to allow the successful candidate to reach their full potential. * Develop new skills and enhance technical ability by working with innovative technologies in a multi\-disciplined environment. * Opportunity to work with large corporate clients on exciting capital projects.

title: Maintenance Engineer
description: **Electrical Maintenance Engineer (Shift\-Based \| 4\-Day Work Week)** **Be part of something being built, not just maintained.** This is a great opportunity to join high\-volume manufacturing operation entering an exciting phase of growth and investment. As part of this, a **new Electrical Maintenance Engineer role** has been created. If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, improving systems, and being trusted to make an impact, this is a role where you won’t just “keep things running”… you’ll help define how they run in the future. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Diagnosing and resolving faults across production and site equipment * Supporting the reliability of automated machinery (e.g. conveyors, packaging systems, processing equipment) * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance and condition monitoring * Responding quickly to breakdowns to minimise downtime * Contributing to continuous improvement and reliability initiatives * Performing root cause analysis and implementing long\-term fixes * Maintaining accurate maintenance records and system updates * Working collaboratively with production and leadership teams * **Playing a key role in the installation, testing, and optimisation of new equipment** **What We’re Looking For** * Qualified in an electrical discipline (time\-served Craft Electrician or equivalent, ) * At least 2 years’ experience in a similar industrial or manufacturing environment * Strong fault\-finding and problem\-solving ability * Comfortable working hands\-on with both electrical and mechanical systems * A proactive mindset you don’t wait to be told, you take ownership * Flexible and team\-oriented approach to shift work * Good communication skills and attention to detail **What’s On Offer** * **€70,000 \+ DOE** * Pension Contribution 5% * 40\-hour week across a 4\-day shift pattern * Annual medical * Full PPE and your own professional toolbox provided **Working Hours \& Shift Pattern** * 4\-day working week (4 shifts over 7 days) * 4 x 10 hour shifts \-3 days off back to back * Start times rotate between: * 4:00am – 2:00pm * 6:00am – 4:00pm * 12:00pm – 10:00pm **Why Apply?** This isn’t a backfill role, you’ll be joining at a time of **real investment and change** . If you want to move beyond reactive maintenance and **get exposure to projects, installs, and improvements** , this is the right opportunity.

title: Mid-Level Full Stack Java Engineer
description: Solance is redefining financial services for the B2B market, with a dedicated focus on family offices and corporate service providers—sectors often overlooked by traditional banks. We are currently in the process of obtaining an EMI (Electronic Money Institution) licence in Ireland, with a long\-term vision to evolve into a fully licensed bank. Our mission is to combine the technological advantages of a neobank with the personalised, high\-touch service of a private bank—delivering exceptional, tailored support to the specific needs of our Clients. **About this role** This is an opportunity to join Solance Europe Limited as an early\-stage engineer in our Dublin city centre based team. You'll report to the CTO and collaborate with the Principal Engineer and the rest of the Product and Tech team members to build a greenfield, cloud\-native banking and payments platform using a modern Java and React native based stack. This is a **hands\-on engineering role** focused on delivering high\-quality features, improving reliability and developer experience, and growing your ownership over services over time. You'll help build a resilient, scalable and secure platform that improves the experience for our **Clients** . You will contribute to building core services, APIs, UI flows, and automated pipelines in a regulated environment—learning best practices in security, resilience, and quality along the way. **Requirements** **Experience** * Track history in deep technical and business curiosity and initiative to deliver value and make things better for their Customers * 2\-4 years of professional experience in software engineering (full stack or strong backend with solid frontend capability). * Experience delivering production software in an agile environment. * Practical experience with NoSQL databases (preferably DynamoDB), including understanding of partition/sort keys, indexing strategies * Strong communicator who collaborates effectively with other engineers and cross\-functional stakeholders. * Proactive, curious, and motivated to learn—able to take ownership of well\-defined areas and expand scope over time. **Technical Skills** * Solid proficiency in **Java** backend development and building **RESTful APIs** . * Practical experience with **React** Native (or closely related modern frontend frameworks). * Working knowledge of **AWS** (deploying/operating services, or building on managed cloud services). * Comfortable with **Git** , code reviews, and collaborative development workflows. **Quality / Operations** * Experience writing automated tests and contributing to reliable release practices. * Familiarity with CI/CD concepts and an interest in automation (build/test/deploy). * Basic understanding of observability (logs/metrics/alerts) and production support practices. **Nice to have** * Exposure to fintech domains such as **payments, onboarding, KYC/AML, wallets, FX, cards** , or regulated environments. * Familiarity with security fundamentals (OWASP, least privilege, encryption in transit/at rest). * Some experience with Python or React Native (helpful but not required). **Benefits** * Join a startup with a bold vision to redefine B2B financial services. * Work on a modern greenfield platform with strong technical guidance and high standards. * Build in a regulated environment and develop valuable experience in security, resilience, and operational excellence. * Make a meaningful impact within the Tech team while growing your own career quickly as part of our early\-stage team.

title: Middle Grade Pharmacist
description: **Job Title: Middle Grade Pharmacist** **Department: Pharmacy Department** **Job Type: Permanent, Full Time** **Hours: 35 hours** **About The Hermitage Clinic Pharmacy Department at Blackrock Health** The Pharmacy department provides an advanced clinical pharmacist service to all wards, supported by our medicines management pharmacy technicians (MMPTs). The dispensary is responsible for dispensing all non\-stock requisitions as well as dispensing Discharge Medication (TTO) prescriptions. Our oncology pharmacists provide a clinical and aseptic preparation service to our substantial oncology service. We are actively involved in E\+T with CBDs presented fortnightly in the department by our team of 17\. **About The Role** The Middle Grade Pharmacist will rotate through a variety of specialities to include surgical, medical, critical care and oncology. This new position requires post\-holders to have at least **one\-year's hospital pharmacy experience** as well as a willingness to commence their post\-graduate diploma if not already undergoing this. This post will have limited dispensary requirements (max 4hrs per week), close support from the Head of Pharmacy and all Senior Pharmacists is provided. **Key Responsibilities** **Leadership** To provide clinical and professional leadership to the pharmacy team and medicines management service To identify and promote best practice To motivate and inspire others (both within Pharmacy and the externally) To proactively develop clinical pharmacy services in line with local and national objectives (for example HSE or NICE guidance) To work collaboratively with pharmacy, medical staff, the multidisciplinary team and management to ensure implementation of local and nationally agreed pharmacy services and objectives To negotiate with and inform clinicians of all grades on issues relating to prescribing practice according to local and national policies **Service Provision** To promote best practice prescribing through co\-ordination of projects and audits in accordance with priorities set by the head of pharmacy or drugs and therapeutic committees. To ensure safe, clinically effective and cost effective use of medicines. To actively contribute to the monitoring and management of drug expenditure through regular analysis and reporting, in order to ensure safe, clinically effective and cost efficient use of drugs. To identify changes in prescribing practices that may impact on drugs budgets To identify new prescribing practices that may impact on the drugs budget (horizon scanning) To be available to pharmacy, medical and nursing staff to answer queries relating to individual patients and the policies or protocols that effect them To attend the Drugs and Thrapeutics committee on an as required basis To undertake audit and investigations and implement changes To present results of audit work at National Meetings if appropriate To develop, implement, monitor and evaluate protocols and guidelines To update protocols and guidelines on a rolling basis To participate in the pharmaceutical aspects of clinical audit to ensure optimal drug usage To liaise with clinicians in smoothing the entry of new drug protocols and ensure old practice is phased out **General Service Provision** To provide a high quality, customer focused pharmaceutical service to all Hermitage Clinic in\-patients To review prescriptions for their clinical appropriateness, safety and legality and conduct medicine reconciliations as per hospital policy To dispense and supply drugs to patients as per service demand To provide evaluated pharmaceutical advice and information to all health care professionals and patients of the hospital using on\-line databases and primary sources To have a working knowledge of pharmacy stock control and ordering systems **Clinical Practice** To act as a clinical role model to all pharmacists and demonstrate the ability to provide safe, clinically effective and cost efficient use of medicines. To professionally screen/check prescriptions, thus taking professional responsibility, as required by the hospital To integrate research evidence into practice To actively seek to improve the ward and clinical services provided To demonstrate expert clinical knowledge To plan, monitor and review drug treatment protocols To ensure that medication histories and patients' own drugs are checked on admission or transfer To enhance the quality of patient care To manage difficult and ambiguous problems To make decisions with limited information To work with a whole\-system patient focused approach To demonstrate advanced level of clinical reasoning and judgement To work with Infectious Diseases/Microbiology consultants to develop best practice protocols **For a full Job Description and Person Specification or for an informal discussion about suitability for the role please contact** **pfoley@hermitageclinic.ie** **(01\-6459845\)** **Please Note:** ***Job descriptions are a reflection of the present service requirements and may be subject to review and amendments to meet the changing needs of the service. Please note that we reserve the right to close this vacancy early if sufficient applications are received.*** ***The Hermitage Clinic is an Equal Opportunities employer, and we are committed to creating an environment that promotes equality and dignity at work.*** ***The Hermitage Clinic is committed to providing reasonable accommodation when needed. Please advise if you have particular requirements, so that we can look into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at HR.hermitage@blackrockhealth.com*** ***It is essential that you have valid work permission for the Republic of Ireland to take up employment for this position, or in the case of critical skills, you must be eligible for a work permit.***

title: Middle Office Operations Associate - Private Credit
description: **Company Overview** KKR is a leading global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR aims to generate attractive investment returns by following a patient and disciplined investment approach, employing world\-class people, and supporting growth in its portfolio companies and communities. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds. KKR’s insurance subsidiaries offer retirement, life and reinsurance products under the management of Global Atlantic Financial Group. References to KKR’s investments may include the activities of its sponsored funds and insurance subsidiaries. **Position Summary** KKR is seeking a Middle Office Operations professional for its Global Investment Operations Team based in the Dublin office. This individual will work and collaborate with the global operations team, as well as employees in other functions and businesses at the firm. This role is a unique opportunity to become part of an evolving and strategic function dedicated to ‘best\-in\-class’ operations management practices to support our global business growth. **Responsibilities** * Preparation and review of NAV and regulatory reporting * Supervision of daily cash and position reconciliations * Investigate and resolve discrepancies between internal systems and trustees/custodians * Manage external service providers * Record and maintain all lifecycle transactions, capital activity, and borrowings in internal systems * Deliver timely, accurate support to external clients and internal stakeholders * Undertake ad hoc project work, **Ideal Experience** A successful candidate will have relevant experience in fund accounting, fund administration or coming from an operations background (credit/asset management, private equity/infrastructure fund industry experience is preferred). Strong knowledge of NAV calculation on fund vehicles and/or Fund of Fund experience would be a distinct advantage. **CRITICAL COMPETENCIES FOR SUCCESS** * Clear intellectual curiosity and commercial acumen with the drive and potential to succeed in a fast\-paced work environment * Self\-starter – someone who enjoys “rolling up his/her sleeves and getting things done”; has high energy; a strong work ethic; displays the ability to work independently; be creative; and entrepreneurial * Grasps key concepts quickly and displays potential to develop greater specialist knowledge * Solid interpersonal and relationship\-building skills – can effectively develop trusted relationships with many constituents, build credibility quickly, and partner effectively with others * Strong execution and project management skills – highly organized, very detail oriented, proactive problem\-solver, solutions\-oriented, and a knack for anticipating obstacles and addressing issues early on * Efficient and effective work style in a dynamic environment with the ability to multi\-task and work across a number of different work\-streams, assignments and/or projects. Proven ability to manage heavy workloads while remaining attentive to details, and prioritizing time efficiently when faced with multiple projects * Focuses on delivering exceptional client service, with a sense of urgency and follow through * Displays maturity, outstanding judgment, critical thinking, sensitivity and creativity to changing needs and situations. Acts professionally regarding sensitive subjects and maintains confidentiality while exercising discretion at all times * Team\-player – willing to pitch\-in when needed to help on projects, tasks and initiatives, as and when needed; enjoys working in a collaborative and collegial environment; humble and enthusiastic with the desire to learn * Strong working knowledge of MS Office, particularly advanced Excel and previous experience with Advent Geneva would be preferable. KKR is an equal opportunity employer. Individuals seeking employment are considered without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, or any other category protected by applicable law. KKR will provide reasonable accommodations as required by applicable federal, state, and/or local laws. Individuals seeking an accommodation for the application or interview process should email Benefits@kkr.com. Emails sent for unrelated issues, such as following up on an application, will not receive a response. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access https://www.kkr.com/careers because of your disability. You can request reasonable accommodations by sending an email to Benefits@kkr.com. Only emails left for this purpose will be returned. **Massachusetts Applicants:** It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. This notice applies only to applicants and employees who work or will work in Massachusetts, in accordance with applicable state law.

title: Monetization Strategy & Operations Associate Graduate (GBS) - 2026 Start (MS/BS)
description: **Responsibilities** Team Introduction We’re looking for an individual with a strong entrepreneurial, strategic and business operational background to join our high performing Monetization Strategy \& Operations team. Our team is responsible for shaping monetization efforts in the region primarily focused on driving revenue growth strategies, sales enablement and efficiency efforts. This role will work with cross\-functional teams to find trends and proactively find solutions to improve processes to enable revenue and customer base growth. You will provide support to our sales team in executing localized go\-to\-market strategies, planning and execution. We stay focused on aligning the highest\-level company priorities with effective day\-to\-day operations, and help evolve early stage ideas into future\-growth initiatives. We are looking for talented individuals to join our team in 2026\. As a graduate, you will get opportunities to pursue bold ideas, tackle complex challenges, and unlock limitless growth. Launch your career where inspiration is infinite at TikTok. Successful candidates must be able to commit to an onboarding date by end of year 2026\. Please state your availability and graduation date clearly in your resume. Candidates can apply to a maximum of two positions and will be considered for jobs in the order you apply. The application limit is applicable to TikTok and its affiliates' jobs globally. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis \- we encourage you to apply early. **Responsibilities** * Perform analyses and conduct internal and external research to inform business strategies. * Support in developing processes and policies related to business needs. * Support and manage initiatives on a project basis, collaborating closely with cross\-functional teams in EMEA and cross\-region where needed. * Anticipate issues and take initiative to solve them through developing strategic project plans and data\-driven analysis. * Assist in the preparation and presentation of reports and findings to senior management and stakeholders. * Conduct market analysis and benchmarking to identify trends and opportunities. * Provide administrative and logistical support for various business projects and events. **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications * Final year or recent graduate. * Ability to perform analyses and conduct internal and external research to inform business strategies. * Experience in supporting the development of processes and policies related to business needs. * Strong project management skills with the ability to manage initiatives on a project basis and collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams. * Proactive problem\-solving skills with the ability to anticipate issues and develop strategic project plans. **Preferred Qualifications:** * Proficiency in data\-driven analysis to support decision\-making processes. * Strong communication skills to effectively collaborate with cross\-functional teams. * Demonstrated ability to take initiative and independently solve problems in a dynamic environment. By submitting an application for this role, you accept and agree to our global applicant privacy policy, which may be accessed here: https://careers.tiktok.com/legal/privacy Job Information **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo. Why Join Us Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day. We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us. Diversity \& Inclusion TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

title: Named Account Executive
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Sales Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. The biggest banks and insurance companies in Poland are under pressure to modernise — and they need a trusted partner to help them do it. That's where you come in. At Salesforce, we're not looking for someone to manage accounts. We're looking for someone to *transform* them. As a Strategic Enterprise Account Executive for Financial Services in Poland, you'll sit at the heart of some of the most consequential technology decisions in the market — shaping 3–5 year transformation agendas for Tier 1 and Tier 2 financial institutions, and building the kind of executive relationships that last. This is a role for people who are energised by complexity, not intimidated by it. **What You'll Be Doing** * Owning a portfolio of Poland's top banking and insurance institutions — not managing it, *owning* it * Building and executing account strategies that go way beyond pipeline hygiene — think multi\-year transformation roadmaps * Leading high\-value, multi\-stakeholder sales cycles with CIOs, CTOs, COOs, and Heads of Digital, Risk, and Distribution * Positioning Salesforce's full platform — Sales, Service, Marketing, Data, and AI — as the answer to real business problems: growth, cost efficiency, regulatory pressure, and customer experience * Orchestrating world\-class internal teams (Solution Engineering, Customer Success, Industry Advisors, Partners) to win big * Landing, expanding, and growing accounts at scale — with pipeline that's real, qualified, and predictable **What You Bring** * 10\+ years in enterprise software or SaaS sales — with a track record of 7\-figure\+ deals * Proven experience selling into banking and/or insurance in Poland *(this one's non\-negotiable)* * Confidence operating at C\-level — you challenge thinking, reframe problems, and hold your ground * A deep understanding of what keeps financial institutions up at night: legacy transformation, regulation, digital channels, cost pressure * High ownership mindset — you don't wait for resources, you pull them in * Fluency in Polish and English **Even better if you have:** * Experience with platform sales (CRM, data, cloud, AI) * An existing network within Polish financial institutions * The ability to spot an opportunity inside an RFP — not just respond to one **What Success Looks Like Here** You're consistently delivering against multi\-million quotas. You're the trusted advisor your clients call before they call anyone else. You're expanding accounts from a single use case into enterprise\-wide adoption. And your forecast? It's one you're proud to stand behind. **Why Salesforce** We're the global \#1 in CRM, Data, and AI — and in Poland's financial services market, our brand opens doors. You'll have access to the broadest platform in the industry, the credibility to walk into any boardroom, and the genuine opportunity to shape large\-scale digital transformation in one of Central Europe's most dynamic markets. We're also a company that believes business is the greatest platform for change — and that the best work happens when everyone feels seen, valued, and empowered to bring their whole self to work. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form . Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. Posting Statement Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Network Development Engineer , European Sovereign Cloud
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- Would you like to be an Engineer that builds the Cloud, rather than an Engineer that just uses it? At Amazon Web Services (AWS) our Network Development Engineers look after the behind\-the\-scenes network and tools that make the world's largest cloud computing infrastructure possible. As a Network Development Engineer you will be responsible for building and operating the network infrastructure where you will learn the tools, services and components that the team relies upon every day. Through close collaboration, support and exchange of ideas, we share a common goal to serve our external customers, with seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own and deliver them. If you are motivated and passionate about the work that you do, care about delivering for your customers, have a love for learning and working with new technologies, then we welcome you to join us. You will be working with the most advanced tools, processes and talented people in the industry to make the Cloud a reality. Key job responsibilities A Network Development Engineer is responsible for engineering, implementation, scalability, operations, reliability, and sustainability of our next\-generation networks. An NDE will utilise their networking skills along with our advanced tools and systems to invent, refine and develop automation and mechanisms to reduce manual operations. Our Engineers think at “Amazon scale” to solve problems in permanent, sustainable, and scalable ways, with an eye towards utilising the best solution over the quickest solution. The work will include: * Leading network projects in the areas of network sustaining engineering, network deployment/implementation, network scaling, technology refresh, best practices application, and/or network optimization * Influencing our operations function, responding to customer impact events and requests, troubleshooting, and fixing and improving the automation that runs our network * Analysing the network using tools and metrics to ensure it is operating correctly, safely and securely * Investigating technical issues scientifically and thoroughly, and assist in fixing complex problems so they don't come back * Successfully planning, delivering and simplifying new systems, processes, and tools to better deliver for our customers * Delivering sustainable and repeatable solutions and processes, optimising and removing bottlenecks * Understanding new and upcoming networking systems and developing ways of safely implementing them into our environment A day in the life An NDE is responsible for ensuring all network fabrics and devices are operating as expected. An NDE will utilise their networking skills along with available tools and services to troubleshoot and resolve any issues that have not been remediated through automation and to identify the root cause. They are expected to invent, refine and develop new automation and tools to reduce manual operations and fulfil the requirements of the business and customers. About the team The Network Development team drives engineering, implementation, scalability, operations, reliability, and sustainability for all of the network fabrics that underpin the software services and platforms used within an AWS isolated region. Top Reasons to join our team: * Collaborate with and learn from the best networking engineers in the world * Get to develop, build and operate networks at the very largest scale, in an organisation that values its engineers and promotes innovation and ownership * Be part of a unique team that’s dedicated to history\-making programmes * Start a career path in one of the largest and most successful tech companies with a near\-endless opportunity to explore and grow Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. **BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Knowledge of IP networking fundamentals and extensive experience in the application of IP protocols * Knowledge of network analysis fundamentals and robust troubleshooting * Experience in major internet routing protocols, specifically BGP and OSPF * Experience with network operating systems such as Cisco IOS and Junos * This role requires you to be a national of an EU member state. **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Knowledge of IT best practice frameworks (ITIL, COBIT) and operational excellence concepts or methodologies * Knowledge of network hardware and packet forwarding architectures * Experience with Unix and scripting in Perl, shell, C or C\+\+ * Experience working with customers to diagnose a problem and work towards resolution Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: Network Security Engineer
description: CCIE Network Security Engineer / Architect (Contract) Location: Near Dublin (Hybrid – 3 days onsite per week) Duration: Initial 12\-month contract Eligibility: No sponsorship available Overview An experienced CCIE\-level Network Security Engineer / Architect is required to support the design and delivery of secure, scalable network infrastructure within a complex, 24/7 enterprise environment. This role will act as the primary interface between Cyber Security and Network Operations, leading the delivery of network\-focused security initiatives and architectural change. The successful candidate will provide third\-level expertise, owning the design and implementation of secure network solutions from concept through to delivery. Key Responsibilities * Act as technical lead for network\-focused cyber security initiatives within a critical operational environment * Design and implement secure, scalable, and resilient network architectures aligned to industry best practice * Lead the delivery of network and security solutions across full project lifecycles * Develop network segmentation strategies aligned to zero\-trust principles * Design and implement enterprise data centre networks, including ACI fabric (tenants, VRFs, bridge domains, EPGs) * Configure and manage next\-generation firewall capabilities, including IPS, threat intelligence, and encrypted traffic visibility * Manage firewall lifecycle activities including upgrades, optimisation, and health monitoring * Design and optimise application delivery solutions, including load balancing and traffic management * Implement high availability and disaster recovery strategies for critical services * Collaborate with application and infrastructure teams to ensure performance, resilience, and secure data flows * Provide third\-level support for complex network and security incidents * Monitor network and security performance to meet service level requirements * Maintain and enhance network services supporting internal and external business operations * Develop and test failover and resilience strategies across critical network components * Mentor and support junior team members * Produce high\-quality technical documentation and support governance processes Skills \& Experience Essential * CCIE certified (mandatory) * Extensive experience in network engineering and security architecture within enterprise environments * Strong expertise in: * TCP/IP networking * LAN, WAN, VPN technologies * Cisco networking and firewall technologies * Network protocols and topologies * Proven experience designing and implementing secure network architectures * Experience with data centre networking, including ACI * Strong understanding of cybersecurity principles, including threat management and vulnerability assessment * Experience working within structured IT service environments (e.g. ITIL) * Excellent communication, documentation, and stakeholder engagement skills Desirable * Experience with Citrix NetScaler (application delivery, load balancing, WAF) * Knowledge of Identity and Access Management (IAM) * Experience with automation tools such as Python, Ansible, or REST APIs * CISSP certification * Experience working in highly regulated or critical infrastructure environments

title: PMO Analyst
description: **About Goodbody** Goodbody is a leading financial services firm, committed to serving and growing client relationships for 150 years. We are a full service, investment\-led business offering wealth management, asset management, investment banking and private equity services. Our staff of over 400 work in offices across Dublin, London, Cork and Galway. We’re proud to offer prospective employees a professional, collegial work environment that encourages diversity of thought and background. Our reputation in the Irish market for putting customers first is as consistent as the company’s long\-standing commitment to giving back to the communities that we work in. **About The PMO Analyst Role** The PMO Analyst provides structured support to the Business Change PMO, helping to ensure that change activity across Goodbody is well governed, accurately reported, and consistently tracked. The role supports existing PMO capability by assisting with governance administration, portfolio reporting, demand tracking, and control activities, enabling the PMO to operate effectively and ensuring decision making forums are supplied with reliable information. **PMO Analyst Accountabilities Include** **Governance, Portfolio \& Change Oversight Support** * Support the coordination and administration of PMO governance forums, including the Change Leadership Committee, Executive Change Council, and Programme/Project Steering Committees. * Assist with the preparation of agendas, papers, and supporting materials. * Capture and maintain meeting minutes, decisions, actions, risks, issues, and dependencies. * Support the maintenance of the enterprise change pipeline, including programme/project registers and tracking of small change, operational, and incident driven requests. * Monitor progress across actions, milestones, and dependencies, highlighting overdue or higher risk items to the PMO Lead where appropriate. **Reporting, Risk \& Data Management** * Collect, consolidate, and maintain data inputs from delivery teams to support: * Status reporting * RAID and dependency tracking * Benefits tracking * Produce draft reports, dashboards, and portfolio views using agreed PMO templates. * Perform basic data quality checks to ensure information is accurate, complete, and timely, flagging gaps or inconsistencies for follow up. * Assist in preparing portfolio, risk, and issue summaries for governance and decision\-making forums. **PMO Standards, Tools \& Knowledge Support** * Support the maintenance and consistent use of PMO governance artefacts, including templates, reporting standards, and core process documentation. * Maintain accurate change and portfolio records in agreed tools (e.g. Azure DevOps), including intake, backlog, and prioritisation views. * Support document version control and structured storage of PMO artefacts. * Assist with the collation of lessons learned, closure documentation, and governance evidence to support internal reviews, audit requests, and knowledge sharing. **Qualifications And Experience** **Essential** * Third level education required; holding relevant qualifications from the IOB or an equivalent qualification is desirable. * 1–3 years’ experience in a PMO, project support, or reporting role * Strong organisational and administrative skills * High attention to detail * Comfortable working with structured trackers and reporting templates * Good Excel skills (tracking, pivots, basic reporting) * Ability to work across multiple initiatives simultaneously **Desirable** * Exposure to governance forums or programme reporting * Familiarity with Azure DevOps or similar tracking tools * Experience in Financial Services or regulated environments **Ideal Characteristics** The ideal candidate is a detail ‑ focused PMO or project support professional with strong organisational and administrative skills, comfortable managing multiple initiatives using structured trackers and reporting templates, with solid Excel capability, clear communication skills, and a dependable, process ‑ oriented approach to supporting governance, reporting, and delivery activities in a regulated or structured change environment. **Employee Value Proposition** Goodbody Is Focused On Helping Our Employees Thrive, And We Do This By Bringing To Life Our Employee Value Proposition (EVP) ‘Together We Prosper’. Our Firm’s Purpose Is To Create Long\-term Prosperity, And We Cannot Execute On Our Purpose Without The Right Talent At Goodbody. To Make Sure We Attract And Retain Industry\-leading, Passionate And Inspiring Talent, We’ve Built a Vibrant Culture Based On Inclusion, Opportunities To Grow And a Commitment To ‘do The Right Thing’, One Of Our Core Values. We Live This By Offering * Regular employee events through our universal inclusion programme fostering a sense of belonging and celebrating differences. And an ‘always on’ approach to social, financial, mental and physical wellbeing. * A benefits package that includes a generous pension scheme, educational sponsorship and health insurance, along with a hybrid working model to promote better work/life balance * Opportunities to give back through sustainability initiatives, volunteering and on\-site and online meetings with our charity partners Goodbody is an equal opportunities employer and is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace which values and benefits from the diversity of our workforce. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process. Should you have a reasonable accommodation request please include a note in your application or email us directly at HR@Goodbody.ie Please read the Goodbody Recruitment Privacy Notice. By submitting your personal data to us, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this Privacy Notice and agree to the use of your personal data in line with this. We will only use the information that we collect about you lawfully in accordance with the Data Protection Acts, 1988 and 2003 and the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. **Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to Goodbody by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role.** Goodbody operates a direct sourcing model and where agency assistance is required, the HR team will engage directly with our recruitment partners. Goodbody Stockbrokers UC, trading as Goodbody, is regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland. In the UK, Goodbody is also subject to regulation by the Financial Conduct Authority. Goodbody is a member of Euronext Dublin and the London Stock Exchange. Goodbody is a member of the group of companies headed by AIB Group plc. For AIB Employees: Please note there are specific controls in place for transfers between AIB and Goodbody. Goodbody is part of AIB Group and you must inform your AIB line manager and HR BP in advance of making an application to Goodbody.

description: The Wright Group is looking for a Procurement \& EPOS Assistant to support our purchasing and EPOS systems across multiple hospitality venues. Key Responsibilities **EPOS Systems Management** * Update and maintain EPOS systems across all hospitality venues. * Manage product file changes including PLUs, menus, categories, pricing, and promotions. * Ensure accuracy between EPOS, back\-office, and stock systems. * Coordinate EPOS rollouts across multiple venues. * Troubleshoot issues and liaise with IT or external providers. * Support new system features and integrations. **Procurement \& Purchasing Support** * Assist with supplier ordering, sourcing, and stock processes. * Maintain accurate purchasing records. * Ensure correct pricing and supplier data in all systems. * Monitor stock levels and support decisions. **Supplier Contract Management** * Maintain and oversee supplier contracts. * Verify compliance with pricing, service levels, deliveries, and obligations. * Escalate discrepancies. * Support contract reviews and renewals. **Internal Controls \& Reporting** * Maintain strong control procedures. * Produce purchasing, stock, and EPOS reports. * Assist audits and compliance checks. * Support continuous improvement initiatives. **Skills, Knowledge \& Experience** **Essential:** * Previous hospitality industry experience (any role). * Strong analytical and numerical skills. * Excellent attention to detail. * Strong organisational and communication abilities. * Willingness to travel across multiple venues. **Desirable:** * Full driving licence. **Why work for the group:** * Employee Assistance Program * Fun staff days out * % off our sister sites * Superb training * Competitive salary * Employee Recognition Award * Employee of the month * Bike to work * Strong company culture * Staff referral program * Great opportunities for career progression

title: Production Operator
description: **BioProcess Associate – Drug Substance Manufacturing** We are seeking a **BioProcess Associate** to join the Drug Substance Manufacturing team within a leading pharmaceutical drug manufacturing company. This role is part of a dynamic and growing production environment focused on delivering high\-quality biopharmaceutical products using advanced manufacturing technologies. **Shift Pattern:** Initially operating Monday–Friday standard office hours, with transition to either a 12\-hour day shift pattern or a 4\-cycle shift pattern to support routine manufacturing operations. **Key Responsibilities** * Perform manufacturing operations including **Upstream Processing (USP), Downstream Processing (DSP), and Buffer/Media Preparation** . * Execute production activities in accordance with approved **SOPs and Manufacturing Batch Records (MBRs)** . * Maintain a strong safety culture and ensure full compliance with **cGMP standards** at all times. * Write, review, and revise **SOPs, OJTs, Production Batch Records, logbooks, training documents, and work instructions** . * Train and mentor team members in process execution, SOP adherence, and equipment operation. * Support achievement of Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) including **Safety, Quality, Schedule Adherence, and Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE)** . * Assist in the investigation of deviations through the Quality Management System (QMS) and implement corrective actions where required. * Participate actively in shift handovers and communicate operational updates to the Shift Lead. * Identify operational challenges and escalate issues within the production area as needed. * Apply Lean manufacturing tools (e.g., Standard Work, 5S, TPM, Gemba, Continuous Improvement initiatives) to enhance operational efficiency. * Support risk assessments, including HAZOP activities, where required. * Collaborate cross\-functionally with Maintenance, Engineering, Quality, and other departments. * Ensure all activities comply with company policies, SOPs, and global regulatory standards including **cGMP, cGLP, and environmental guidelines** . * Support equipment commissioning, qualification, and transition from development to commercial manufacturing. **Required Skills \& Experience:** * Experience in **bioprocessing, upstream processing, or downstream processing** within the pharmaceutical or biopharmaceutical industry. * Strong understanding of **cGMP regulations, safety standards, and environmental compliance** . * Experience executing operations in accordance with SOPs, batch records, and manufacturing control systems. * Ability to accurately document activities in real time using electronic or manual systems. * Experience working in **controlled cleanroom environments (Grade C, Grade D, or CNC)** is desirable. * Strong attention to detail, documentation skills, and problem\-solving capability. * Ability to work independently and as part of a shift\-based manufacturing team. * Adaptable and comfortable working in a fast\-paced production environment.

title: Project Analyst
description: **Silent Aire** **Dublin** **What You Will Do** The Project Analyst is responsible for managing the financial performance of assigned projects by ensuring accurate cost tracking, timely reporting, and effective financial controls. This role works closely with project managers, operations teams, Plant Controller and senior leadership to support budgeting, forecasting, billing, and compliance activities. The Project Analyst ensures that all project financials are accurate, transparent, and aligned with company standards and client requirements. **How You Will Do It** Project Financial Management * Monitor, track, and analyze project costs, budgets, and financial performance * Prepare Bi\-Weekly project cost reports, variance analyses, and financial summaries * Maintain accurate job costing, including labor, materials, equipment, and subcontractor expenses * Support project managers in understanding financial results and cost drivers Billing \& Revenue * Prepare and process progress billings, milestone invoices, and time‑and‑materials billing * Review contracts to ensure billing aligns with terms, rates, and deliverables * Track accounts receivable and unbilled accounts * Ensure revenue recognition complies with company policies and accounting standards * Ensure jobs are reconciled and closed in a timely manner. Budgeting \& Forecasting * Assist in developing project budgets and financial forecasts * Update forecasts based on actual performance and projected costs * Identify cost overruns, risks, and opportunities for savings Sales \& Backlog * Assist in the backlog reporting by region * Assist in secured weekly reporting by region * Work with Sales team to ensure sales force is updated correctly * Assist in the backlog flow for accurate reporting. Compliance \& Documentation * Maintain accurate project financial records and documentation * Ensure compliance with internal controls, audit requirements, and client reporting standards * Support internal and external audits related to project financials Collaboration \& Communication * Work closely with project managers, procurement, and operations teams * Provide financial insights to support decision‑making * Communicate project financial results clearly and professionally to stakeholders * Work closely with the Plant Controller to ensure alignment. **What We Look For** * Diploma or degree in Accounting, Finance, or Business Administration * 2–5\+ years of experience in project accounting or cost control * Strong understanding of project accounting principles and job‑costing methods * Proficiency with accounting software (Sage300, SAP, or similar) * Excel skills (pivot tables, lookups) * Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills * Ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines * Strong communication and problem‑solving abilities * Experience in the Manufacturing or Construction industry preferred **\#AVRIL**

title: Project Delivery Engineer
description: Elusav Recruitment has an excellent opportunity for a Projects Delivery Engineer for a leading Engineering and Technical Services Contractor in the water and wastewater treatment space. They are looking for a Projects Delivery Engineer who can support and manage a diverse range of design projects, both greenfield and brownfield, as well as support preconstruction/proposal activities. This role sits within the company's contracting division, but they also operate large managed services contracts for Ireland's largest WWTP facilities, where the majority of the projects are delivered. The Projects Delivery Engineer will report to the Senior Projects managers and support the delivery of critical projects up to the value of €40 Million. Excellent opportunity to join an industry leader with a significant footprint in the wastewater treatment space in Ireland. Long term local opportunity, and also strong remuneration package. **Responsibilities:** * Production of documentation, including reports, drawings, calculations, specifications, tender documentation, and particular specifications. * Production of project budgets and resource\-loaded detailed work programmes. * Co\-ordinating with PSCS, PSDP, Client, Operations and other stakeholders. * Providing technical advice and guidance. * Providing input to bids and project managing appropriate projects. * Supervision \& management of contractors. * Responding to Client requests for information. * Preparation and/or review RAMS. * Identify interfaces with operations, impacts, consequences, work\-arounds and plan, execute operations\-dependent tasks within the suitable window. * Support maintenance by managing SLAs and contractors. * Identify and prioritise capital maintenance projects based on equipment/process condition and criticality. **Requirements:** * Good knowledge of techniques for planning, monitoring and controlling projects. * Ability to risk assess equipment and criticality * Have experience in design at all levels, from feasibility to a "for construction" level. * Have considerable technical knowledge of all aspects of design as it impacts technical, budgetary and programme issues. * PRINCE2 or PMP qualification or experience in a PRINCE2/PMP structured project is preferred. **Contact:** If you would like to learn more about the vacancy, apply now, or contact Brendan Mullins at bmullins@elusav.com or \+353 61 527 150\.

title: Psychologist / Psychotherapist - Aware Clinical Services
description: **Job Overview:** We are seeking a compassionate, innovative, and enthusiastic psychologist or psychotherapist to join our friendly and dedicated team in the NGO sector. This is a new role offering an exciting opportunity to support the strategic clinical leadership across the range of services offered and shape high\-quality mental health service delivery for people living with depression, bipolar, and anxiety. **About Aware:** Aware is the national organisation providing free support, education, information and therapeutic services to people impacted by depression, bipolar and anxiety. We play a vital role in offering free mental health services across the country to the public. Aware works with a large team of mental health professionals and volunteers and training partners to deliver our wide range of vital services. **Our Vision** Aware undertakes to create a society where people affected by stress, depression, bipolar disorder and mood related conditions are understood, supported, free from stigma, and are encouraged to access appropriate therapies. **Our Values** Excellence, Compassion, Accountability and Integrity. **About the role** Reporting to Aware’s Clinical Director (Senior Clinical Psychologist), key aspects of the role include (but are not limited to): * To ensure the clinical effectiveness of Aware counselling and psychoeducation programmes by supporting clinical audit and other service development activities related to the services * To contribute to creating a psychologically informed framework of service delivery across Aware Services * To provide clinical consultation/guidance and, where required, clinical supervision to Aware clinicians and service managers * To undertake direct assessment and triage with new service users to the Aware Counselling Service, in line with best practice * To hold a small caseload, offering brief psychological therapy to adults presenting with mild to moderate depression and anxiety * To conduct risk assessments and implement risk management plans as appropriate * To contribute to research in line with the Aware Research Strategy and overall objectives * To plan and deliver teaching and training on workplace mental health workshops in line with best practice and current evidence base * To represent Aware in public, policy, and sector\-wide mental health discussions * To work with the communications department utilising the sector wide research / insights to inform our evidence\-informed communications strategy * Support the Clinical Director in offering external\-facing psychological leadership, specifically engaging state bodies and medical stakeholders to represent, promote, and advance Aware strategic objectives. **Essential Skills and Experience:** * Have an Irish post\-graduate professional psychology qualification (Doctorate or equivalent) accredited by the Psychological Society of Ireland in Clinical or Counselling Psychology. Candidates qualified overseas must provide evidence of validation by the Department of Health at the time of application. *Or* * Psychotherapist fully accredited by the Irish Council for Psychotherapy (ICP) or the Irish Association for Counselling and Psychotherapy (IACP) **For psychologists** \- at least 5 years’ experience in professional psychology, with no less than 3 years spent in gaining the post graduate qualification and at least 1 year full time in post qualification experience as a professional psychologist. **For psychotherapists** \- a minimum of five years post qualification / accreditation experience working in health and social care, therapeutic or related organisational settings supporting people with depression and anxiety *And* * Commitment to evidence\-based practice and client\-centred care * Effective presentation and facilitation skills including the ability to present information in a clear and concise manner to a wide range of audiences * Excellent communication, teamwork, and interpersonal skills * Comfortable with a wide range of IT systems **Desirable:** * Experience of the not\-for\-profit sector * Experience of delivering and evaluating brief therapeutic interventions * Interest in service development and creation of psychoeducational and therapeutic resources in mental health. **Package** This is a permanent post. Salary will be commensurate with qualifications and experience. The salary range for this role will be **€52,000 – €60,000 pro rata (0\.6 FTE / 22\.5 hours per week)** * Aware is an equal opportunities employer. * The role is office based in 9 Upper Leeson Street, Dublin 4, offering a hybrid work model To apply please submit **a one\-page cover letter outlining your suitability for the role and a CV (2 pages max)** to info@aware.ie *before 5pm on 12th May*. *Informal enquiries to Dr Susan Brannick on 01 661 7211* Pay: €52,000\.00\-€60,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension Work Location: Hybrid remote in DUBLIN 4, County Dublin (D04\)

title: Quantitative Analyst- Financial Risk Model Validation, Dublin.
description: At AIB, our values guide how we work and how we support each other. We’re looking for someone who puts **Customer First** , takes initiative and **Owns the Outcome** , and is always looking for ways to **Eliminate Complexity** . You’ll treat colleagues and customers with fairness and **Show Respect** , and you’ll thrive in a culture built on collaboration where we **Be One Team** to deliver meaningful impact. **Location/Office Policy:** Molesworth St, Dublin 2\. Hybrid (3 days per week in the office) * Are you interested in applying your quantitative skills to solve financial problems? * Are you interested in developing your skills and getting exposure to a range of financial risk models including derivative pricing models, risk measurement models and behavioural models? * Are you comfortable working as part of a small collaborative team to complete model validation projects and to prepare and present reports on these projects? **What Is The Role** Quantitative analyst for the Financial Risk Model Validation Team. The focus of this role is on validating financial risk models. Key requirements are a familiarity with mathematical modelling and financial risk software, and knowledge of the different types of financial products and risks in the banking industry. **Key Accountabilities;** * Preparing, documenting and presenting validation reports for financial risk models including derivative valuation models, risk measurement models and behavioural models. * Working with model developers and other key stakeholders to track validation actions to completion. * Providing quantitative support to the wider Financial Risk team. **What you Will Bring;** * Relevant third\-level qualification or postgraduate qualification in an analytical discipline, e.g., chemistry, engineering, mathematics, physics. * Completion, or progression towards completion, of a relevant professional qualification, e.g. PRM, FRM or CFA. * Work experience in the financial industry, with a strong focus on financial risk models, e.g., derivatives valuation, VaR and xVA models. * Coding experience in Python or R. Knowledge of Calypso, FINCAD or QRM will be an advantage. **Why Work For AIB** We are committed to offering our colleagues choice and flexibility in how we work and live and our hybrid working model enables our people to balance their time between working from home and their designated office, subject to their role, the needs of our customers and business requirements. *Some of our benefits include;* * Market leading Pension Scheme * Healthcare Scheme * Variable Pay * Employee Assistance Programme * Family leave options * Two volunteer days per year Please click **here** for further information about AIB’s PACT – Our Commitment to You. **Key Capabilities** **Behavioural Capabilities:** * Collaborates * Eliminates Complexity * Ensures Accountability **Technical Capabilities** * Risk Analysis * Risk Modelling and Scenario Analysis If you are not sure about your suitability based on any aspects of the role advertised, we encourage you to please contact the Nicole Pasquetti for this role, at careers@aib.ie for a conversation. AIB is an equal opportunities employer, and we pride ourselves on being the first bank in Ireland to receive the Investors in Diversity Gold Standard accreditation from the Irish Centre for Diversity. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for applicants and employees. Should you have a reasonable accommodation request please email the Talent Acquisition team at careers@aib.ie **Disclaimer** Unsolicited CVs sent to AIB by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this position. AIB operates a direct sourcing model and where agency assistance is required, the Talent Acquisition team will engage directly with our recruitment partners. Closing Date: 20/05/2026

title: Quantity Surveyor | Specialist Contractor | West Dublin
description: **Job Title:** Quantity Surveyor **Location:** West Dublin **Salary:** €65,000 \- €75,000 \+ package (DOE) **Job Type:** Full\-time, Permanent **About the Role** A well\-established contractor is seeking an experienced Quantity Surveyor to join its West Dublin team. With a strong pipeline of projects across Leinster, this role offers excellent project exposure and a clear path to Senior Quantity Surveyor. You will take full commercial responsibility for projects, working closely with Project Managers and site teams to ensure cost control and successful delivery. **Key Responsibilities** * Manage projects commercially from pre\-construction to final account * Procure and manage subcontract packages * Prepare and agree subcontract documentation and payments * Monitor design changes and assess cost impact * Identify value engineering opportunities * Submit and agree monthly valuations * Produce cost reports, forecasts, and cost vs value analysis * Support final account agreement * Provide commercial support to project teams * Build strong relationships with stakeholders * Mentor junior QS staff as needed **Requirements** * Degree in Quantity Surveying or related discipline * 3\+ years' experience with a main contractor or developer * Strong subcontract and commercial management experience * Good knowledge of construction contracts * Strong communication and analytical skills * Proficient in MS Office; Bluebeam, CostX, or Cubit beneficial * Ability to work in a busy environment **What's on Offer** * €65,000 \- €75,000 \+ package (DOE) * Strong pipeline of Dublin\-based projects * Clear progression to Senior QS * Ongoing training and development * Opportunity for real project ownership

title: Rack Decomissioning Technician , DCC Communities, DECOM
description: **Description** The Decommissioning Technical team is responsible for processing and facilitating large scale legacy Data Center equipment through a liquidation process to ensure that targeted equipment is ready to be removed off premises. Team members will also process large volumes of customer data to ensure that all customer data is sanitized and properly managed. This position requires candidates to be a self\-starter, highly motivated individual who can adhere to strict processes and be able to perform repetitive tasks with no deviation and demonstrate continuously consistent results. Team Members will be working in a team environment not only with other members of the team but also from other teams and must be able to communicate and promote cross\-team functionality. This is an entry level position and qualified candidates do not require intimate details of software operations or networking protocols. Amazon Data Centers takes customer data with the utmost importance, and selected candidates must be able to perform repetitive tasks without variance and adhere to strict Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) that ensures customer data is protected and properly handled. Key job responsibilities **Data Center Technicians (Decommission Technician) Will Be Responsible For** Decommissioning and processing of legacy hardware out of the data center facilities Moving/lifting packages usually no greater than 75 lbs. Unpacking/repacking fragile electronics Relocating and moving of large enterprise equipment Creating/attaching labels and barcodes Hard drive installation and removal, and destruction Basic Networking removal/installation and clearing of configurations. Physical layer and Application layer Updating progress through a web ticketing system Reporting of processes and regular status updates Documenting procedures Escalating to senior technicians and management as needed/required **Familiarity With Basic PC Components/hardware** * HDD/SSD/Flash/Removable storage media * USB/FireWire/Esata * Ram/CPU/Motherboard/Input\-Output devices There may be an element of shift based work required with this role. A day in the life AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. title: Reception Team Member
description: Staycity is an urban aparthotel brand, blending the freedom of apartment living with the quality and consistency of a hotel stay. With locations in top European cities like Dublin, London, Paris, and Edinburgh, we provide home comforts in prime city spots — ideal for the modern traveller. Our mission is to help guests experience the city on their own terms — combining independence and hospitality in every stay. We’re a team of doers, thinkers, and hospitality lovers, committed to delivering standout guest experiences and building a culture we’re proud of. We’re now hiring a **Reception Team Member** to help shape the Staycity guest journey. **Benefits:** * Paid family leave (\>1 year of service) * Flexible working patterns * Bonus scheme that rewards high performers;\- based on our core values and tied to individual property goals * Discounted rates for overnight stays for you, your family and friends * Refer and earn scheme \- earn up to £/€550 * Cycle to work scheme\- to support a healthy lifestyle and our planet * Education Support to help you foster new skills * Volunteer days: 2 paid volunteer days per year * Support for you and your family when you need it with our Employee Assistance Program (EAP) * An extra day off to celebrate your birthday **We would love you to have:** * Previous experience working in a similar property is helpful but not a “must have” as full training will be provided * Prior experience using Opera is helpful but not a “must have” as full training will be provided * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills are required for this role. **What you can do for us:** * Be a Brand Ambassador: Embrace your role with joy, passion, and enthusiasm as you represent Staycity Group * Make a good first impression with all our guests: Confidence and great communication are vital in leaving lasting impressions. * Amaze our guests: Show attention to detail and outstanding organisational skills to delight our guests. * Go above and beyond for guests and team: Create raving fans by always exceeding guest and team expectations. * Bring a positive attitude: Radiate positivity and contribute to a vibrant workplace and team * Be kind \& treat everyone with respect: Strive for an atmosphere of kindness and mutual respect among colleagues and guests **Join us and be part of the journey.**

description: **Job Ref** 653936 **Job Title** Receptionist **Pub** Keavan\`s Port Hotel, Camden Street **Salary** 14\.86 **Closing Date** 12\-05\-2026 **Vacancy Description** The ideal applicant We\`re looking for fun, enthusiastic, passionate people to join our hard\-working teams. As a UK Top Employer with an award\-winning training programme, we know that we can teach you the skills you\`ll need to succeed.
