title: Technicians at Audi North Dublin
description: In line with 2026 growth plans, we are now looking to recruit qualified and experienced Technicians to join our talented team at Audi North Dublin. A full and clean Irish or EU driving licence and applicants must be eligible to work full time hours in the EU. **About The Group** Joe Duffy Group is Ireland’s leading motor retailer with a team of 650 employees and an enviable franchise portfolio of world\-leading brands. In 2023, Joe Duffy Group was announced as Ireland 9th Best Employer in the Sunday Independent. Our competitive advantage is underpinned by the quality of the people we employ and the unrivalled career path we can offer. We are looking to recruit a dynamic and ambitious individual that displays drive, passion, energy, talent and integrity. **Job Purpose** To diagnose, repair and service the range of Audi products in accordance with Audi’s work specifications, ensuring each customer receives excellent customer service. **Key Responsibilities** * Through the use of diagnostic equipment and a systematic approach, ensure vehicle faults are diagnosed and rectified. Meet with customers to identify and clearly understand problems as and when required. * Ensure full understanding of latest technology introduced on the vehicle range and how to diagnose and repair faults on them. * Carry out diagnosis, repairs and servicing to the highest standards and to the appropriate specification. * Ensure all work is carried out observing safe working practices of self and others. * Liaise and seek advice from colleagues and supervisors in situations that are over and above work specified. * Check level and quality of work through use of diagnostic equipment and road testing vehicles after work has been carried out. * Liaise with Service Advisors. * Complete relevant service documentation (Job sheets, PDI’s, service reports) in line with HSO, warranty and Audi Dealer requirements. * Ensure that workshop housekeeping standards are maintained at all times including the use of special and personal tools. * Work with other workshop and Audi Dealer colleagues to provide excellent customer service. title: Transfer Agency Client Services Analyst

Please note that it is a requirement of your continued employment, that you are fit to work night shifts and your employment may be terminated, if you are unable to do so.**

title: UX Researcher
description: **We are looking for our future UX Researcher to join our Product teams in Dublin and our Global Customer Experience (CX) team!** **The role is offered as hybrid with 2 to 3 days per week onsite, in our Sandyford office.** As a UX Researcher on the Experience Insights (EXI) team (which is part of the larger Customer Experience team), you will work closely with designers, product managers and other stakeholders to create and refine digital experiences that improve the lives of our users. Using mixed\-methods research, you’ll support teams by bringing clarity to ambiguity with user insights throughout the product development process, from early\-stage discovery to launch and evaluation. The role is essential in delivering seamless and intuitive user experiences that are grounded in user needs. **What the role entails :** *Product Team Influence* * Develop research plans with clear objectives, participant screeners, moderator guides, recruiting strategies, and timelines * Share research findings with product, design, and executive teams in ways that support decisions * Provide regular project updates, including status, blockers, and open questions * Recommend appropriate research methods and tools based on stakeholder goals * Work with Product Managers to forecast and prioritize research needs *Organizational Influence:* * Join cross\-functional initiatives, representing the user voice and helping to drive continuous improvement * Build user empathy across the organization through readouts and direct feedback *UXR Influence:* * Stay current on industry trends and best practices in UX research **What You Bring To The Role** *Empathy and User Advocacy :* * You center users in your work. You understand their experiences, emotions, and motivations, and you make sure others understand them too. You use interviews, surveys, and usability tests to uncover what matters most. *Analytical Thinking :* * You are confident in both qualitative and quantitative methods and know when to use each. You turn findings into clear, actionable insights that guide decisions. *Communication and Collaboration* * You present insights in a clear and engaging way. You work closely with cross\-functional teams to make sure research findings lead to meaningful improvements. *Technical Proficiency* * You know how to select and use research tools to manage studies, analyze data, and communicate results. You apply these tools effectively in your day\-to\-day work. *Creativity* * You try new approaches when needed and adapt your communication for different audiences. You help your team explore fresh ways to apply research in their work. **Qualifications and Experience :** *Required:* * 3\-5 years of UX research experience, ideally in health tech or regulated industries * Comfortable conducting research in a fast\-paced, cross\-functional environment * Portfolio that shows past research work and deliverables * Experience with tools such as UserZoom, UserTesting, Qualtrics, Confluence, and Miro or Whiteboard * Strong communication and collaboration skills * Comfortable using AI tools as part of the research workflow * Degree in Human\-Computer Interaction, Psychology, Anthropology, or a related field * Willingness and ability to travel internationally *Preferred:* * Experience working in healthcare or medical devices * Experience conducting research on hardware/physical products **Additional information :** * At Resmed, all employees benefit from a bonus plan, the percentage of which depends on your position within the organisation. * Competitive benefits * Working from home flexibility * You also have access to a referral bonus and to Resmed's preferred shareholding programme * Internal career opportunity \- joining an international fast\-pace and massively growing company **OK, so what's next?** Joining us is more than saying “yes” to making the world a healthier place. It’s discovering a career that’s challenging, supportive and inspiring. Where a culture driven by excellence helps you not only meet your goals, but also create new ones. We focus on creating a diverse and inclusive culture, encouraging individual expression in the workplace and thrive on the innovative ideas this generates. If this sounds like the workplace for you, apply now! Joining us is more than saying “yes” to making the world a healthier place. It’s discovering a career that’s challenging, supportive and inspiring. Where a culture driven by excellence helps you not only meet your goals, but also create new ones. We focus on creating a diverse and inclusive culture, encouraging individual expression in the workplace and thrive on the innovative ideas this generates. If this sounds like the workplace for you, apply now! We commit to respond to every applicant.

title: Vice President, Accounting
description: Vice President, Accounting At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day, our teams harness cutting\-edge AI and breakthrough technologies to collaborate with clients, driving transformative solutions that redefine industries and uplift communities worldwide. Recognized as a top destination for innovators, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we power the future of finance – and this is what \#LifeAtBNY is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary. We’re seeking a future team member for the role of Vice President, Accounting to join our Finance/Accounting team. This role is located in Dublin, Ireland. In this role, you’ll make an impact in the following ways: * Lead the preparation and review of financial statements, ensuring accuracy and compliance with relevant accounting standards by demonstrating expertise in the field. * Develop and implement accounting procedures to enhance operational efficiency and ensure robust internal controls. * Provide insightful financial analysis and reporting to support strategic decision\-making and business planning initiatives. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to align accounting practices with organizational objectives and regulatory requirements. * Mentor and guide junior team members, fostering a culture of continuous learning and professional development. * Stay abreast of industry trends and regulatory changes to ensure compliance and inform strategic planning. At BNY, we are focused on developing AI capabilities across the firm in a responsible and controlled manner. The successful candidate will have opportunities to enhance their AI skills and apply them in support of Finance transformation * To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following: * Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. * ACA, ACCA, CMA or equivalent professional certification preferred. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex financial data. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to build strategic relationships and effectively collaborate with cross\-functional teams. Leadership skills with a focus on mentoring and developing team members. * At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at: BNY Newsroom BNY LinkedIn Here’s a few of our recent awards: America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2025 World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune 2025 “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2025 Our Benefits and Rewards: BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay\-for\-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter. BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer \- Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans.

title: Vodafone Sales Associate
description: We are looking for a competitive **Vodafone** **Sales Associate** to help customers identify and purchase products they desire. This Sales Associate duties include selling, restocking, and merchandising. The goal is to provide high class customer service and to increase the company's growth and revenue through sales maximization. This is a **full time** position based in our store in **Gorey, Co. Wexford**. **Who are we?** The Phone Stores are an exclusive sales partner for **Vodafone** operating an extensive network of stores and a field sales team in Leinster and Munster in Ireland. We are looking for dedicated sales representatives to sell the full portfolio of Vodafone’s fixed and mobile products. This sales role will provide you with an **opportunity to sell a portfolio of market leading products** . As a Sales Associate your focus will be sales delivery and supporting your team in achieving team targets. **Responsibilities** * Ensuring elevated levels of customer satisfaction through excellent sales service * Maintaining outstanding store condition and visual merchandising standards * Maintain a fully stocked store * Ascertain customers' needs and wants * Recommend and display items that match customer needs * Welcome and greet customers * Manage point\-of\-sale processes * Actively involve in the receiving of new shipments * Keep up to date with product information * Accurately describe product features and benefits * Follow all companies' policies and procedures **Requirements and Skills** * Proven working experience in sales * Basic understanding of sales principles and customer service practices * Proficiency in English * Track record of over\-achieving sales quota * Solid communication and interpersonal skills * Customer service focus * Friendly, helpful, confident, and engaging personality * Basic administration skills * High school degree **Benefits** * **€14\.15 p/h \+ Commissions** * Continual training courses to enhance your Career Progression Plans * Employee Assistance Program available for both you and your family * Generous Family and Friends Mobile and Fixed Monthly Discounts * Multiple career progression opportunities across our business Job Type: Full\-time Pay: From €14\.15 per hour Work Location: In person

title: Volunteer Online as a Conversation Partner
description: ENGin pairs English\-fluent volunteers from around the world with Ukrainian learners for weekly online conversation practice Participants are matched 1\-on\-1 based on preferences, interests, and availability to ensure an effective and mutually enjoyable communication experience. After a match is made, ENGin supports learners and volunteers throughout their participation in the program with tips, resources, and problem resolution. We screen every learner and volunteer to ensure their fit for the program, and provide training and orientation. Since launching in 2020, ENGin has connected over 26,000 Ukrainians with over 24,000 volunteers. 99% of our learners report breaking the language barrier after 3 months of participation in our program. Want to learn more about volunteering with ENGin? Register to attend one of our weekly Zoom info sessions https //calendly.com/volunteer\-training/volunteer\-info\-sessions You can learn more about ENGin at www.enginprogram.org/volunteer . If you have any questions before signing up, please contact us at info@enginprogram.org. , **Ready to change a life from the comfort of home?** Join ENGin as a conversation partner and be matched with a learner in Ukraine for weekly one\-on\-one English conversations. In just one hour a week, you can help someone build confidence, break the language barrier, and feel supported, all while discovering a new culture and making a genuine human connection. We’ll pair you up with a learner in Ukraine for weekly one hour videochats at times that are convenient for you. During your conversations, you’ll facilitate English practice for your partner, learn about each other’s cultures, and offer an empathetic, authentic space for connection. Our only requirements for volunteers are English fluency, a strong internet connection, and the ability to commit to **one hour a week** for a minimum of 12 weeks. No prior tutoring experience or knowledge of Ukrainian language is needed to volunteer with ENGin. We offer ample training resources, session materials, and total flexibility to customize your sessions to fit your/your partner’s goals and preferences. Our friendly team is available to support you throughout your time with us. Since our launch in 2020, ENGin has matched over 30,000 volunteers with Ukrainians for authentic English\-language connection across borders, and we continue to grow every day. 99% of our learners report feeling they were able to break the language barrier as a participant in our program, and 79% of our participants form lasting friendships. We're incredibly proud of these results, and we can't wait to welcome you into this global community of volunteers making them possible. **Here's What Three ENGin Volunteers Say About Their Experience.** * "I made a friend in Ukraine and we are teaching each other about our culture, our language, and our humor. We're even trading dad jokes! Both of us look forward to our chats and have a hard time wrapping it up." * “Fantastic organization doing great work. So happy to be a small part of it. My partner and I have worked together for two years and have become good friends! Her English has become better, and I have become more knowledgeable about Ukraine!” * "This program has been, I think, life\-changing for both of us. I really consider her more like my daughter right now. That's how close we have become!" **Ready to Get Started?** To learn more about ENGin and become a volunteer, please visit **www.enginprogram.org/volunteer** . **Still curious what volunteering with ENGin looks like?** Watch our short Introduction to Volunteering video to learn more about ENGin and the role of Conversation Partners. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact our team at info@enginprogram.org.

title: Waiter / Waitress
description: **Company Description** The Hoxton has just opened its very first hotel in Ireland in the beautiful capital city of Dublin. This coastal city is steeped in history, packed with green parks and brought to life through music, art and the best social eating and drinking. The Hoxton, Dublin is home to 129 bedrooms of varying sizes, a lobby bar, historic bar, large ground floor restaurant and lobby restaurant, in addition to another first for us \- a nightclub. **Job Description** **What you’ll do…** * Simply put \- you’re the face of our guests’ experience and you bring your own flavour to the(ir) table, making sure that from the moment they arrive to when they leave, they’re having a great time. * Work with the F\&B team in keeping things running smoothly. * Create a fun and relaxed atmosphere where guests come to wind down and enjoy themselves. * Provide a genuinely welcoming and memorable experience for our guests, from a Hox welcome, to handling any questions and helping out with requests. * Anticipate needs and proactively provide solutions so that our guests’ expectations are not only met but exceeded. **Qualifications** **What we’re looking for…** * Individuals. You’re looking for a place where you can be you; no clones in suits here. * Passion for hospitality – whether that’s a drink, a meal, an event or a bed for the night. * You’re all about having a positive impact on the people you interact with, leaving them with a memorable experience. * You’re not precious. We leave our egos at the door and help get things done. * You’re up for doing things differently and trying (almost) everything once. * If we got stuck in a lift together, we’d have a good time and share a few laughs. * You want to be part of a team that works hard, supports each other, and has fun along the way. * If you have previous experience, great! But it’s not a must\-have for us; it’s how you contribute to the team that matters. * Nothing is an issue for you, you know what our guests want even before they do. **Additional Information** **What's in it for you...** * Become part of a team that’s very passionate about creating great hospitality experiences. * Competitive salary. * 30 days holiday (including bank holidays), pension and life insurance. * Food on us during your shift. * Enjoy a free night at The Hoxton and a meal for two when you first start with us. * Training to get you settled into your role with ongoing development that helps you think, make and thrive to set you up for success at work. * Time off to volunteer with one of our partner charities. * Hox Hero and Nifty Fifty, our rewards for going the extra mile and living our values. * An annual diversity and inclusion calendar of events creating opportunities for you to learn, celebrate and make a positive impact. * Regular team get togethers, from our High Fives to our (pretty special!) team parties – we know how to have a good time! * The chance to challenge the norm and work in an environment that is both creative and rewarding. * Join an innovative, fast\-growing, international group that is shaking things up on the hospitality scene. * Excellent discounts across The Hoxton and the global Ennismore family.

title: Warehouse Associate
description: **WHO WE ARE:** EOS IT Solutions is a Global Technology and Logistics company, providing Collaboration and Business IT Support services to some of the world’s largest industry leaders, delivering forward\-thinking solutions based on multi\-domain architecture. Customer satisfaction and commitment to superior quality of service are our top business priorities, along with investing in and supporting our partners and employees. We are a true International IT provider and are proud to deliver our services through global simplicity with trusted transparency **THE POSITION** Based at EOS IT Solutions' facility in Swords, the Warehouse Associate is primarily responsible for ensuring that day\-to\-day operational logistics activities run effectively and efficiently. This includes cycle counting, inventory receipting, and processing of all inbound and outbound shipments. The Warehouse Associate will work as part of a dynamic and growing team to deliver EOS suppliers’ and customers with an unrivalled service and contribute to our growth and success. **What You Will Do** * Ensuring the day\-to\-day operational Logistics activities run smoothly and efficiently. * Loading and unloading trucks, cycle counting, kitting, picking, packing, shrink wrapping, labelling, and palletizing products. * Support teams with internal moves, additions, and changes. * Receiving and processing all equipment shipments. * Responsible for performing the physical or administrative/physical tasks storing, and distributing of products, materials, parts, supplies and equipment. * Sorting and placing materials or items on racks, shelves, or in bins according to pre\-determined sequence such as size, type, style, colour, or product code. * Examines stocks and distributes materials in inventory. * Performs other duties and responsibilities when needed. **ESSENTIAL CRITERIA:** * Excellent follow through skills, coupled with strong organizational skills and solid communication and interpersonal skills. * Ready, willing, and able to deliver excellence independently or collaboratively. * Basic knowledge of warehouse safety and operations. * Be an analytical thinker who can help organize and complete tasks. * Be innovative and self\-motivated, with a keen ability to ask questions, as well as a self\-directed learner. * Excellent interpersonal skills, including relationship building and collaboration within a diverse, cross\-functional team. * Ability to pay meticulous attention to detail. * Able to lift heavy boxes and equipment. **DESIRABLE CRITERIA:** * Previous experience working in a Warehouse or similar environment * Forklift License \- preferably CB/Aisle Master * Possess experience with inventory management. **BELONGING AT EOS:** *EOS is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We invite you to consider opportunities at EOS regardless of your gender; gender identity; gender reassignment; age; religious or similar philosophical belief; race; national origin; political opinion; sexual orientation; disability; marital or civil partnership status or other non\-merit factor.* \#IND

title: Warehouse Operative
description: Are you a dedicated and experienced warehouse professional who thrives in a collaborative work environment and takes pride in upholding high standards? If so, an exciting opportunity awaits you at HuntOffice as a Warehouse Operative. **Main Responsibilities:** * Team Player: Play an integral role in ensuring seamless warehouse operations. * Procedural Adherence: Adhere to goods receiving and dispatch procedures. * Picking and Packing: Maintain standards in picking and packing. * Inventory Support: Assist in cycle counting and stock checks. * Performance Excellence: Meet daily SLA and KPI targets consistently. * Safety First: Adhere to health and safety policies at all times. * Efficient Handling: Safely load and unload goods. * Order Organisation: Organise orders and manifests for van routes. * Issue Reporting: Promptly report any delivery and collection issues. **Requirements:** * Punctuality and Reliability: Exhibit excellent timekeeping and attendance. * Safety and Cleanliness: Contribute to maintaining a safe and clean warehouse. * Experience: Possess 2\-3 years of warehousing experience * Communication and Organisation: Showcase strong communication and organisational skills. * Tech Proficiency: Demonstrate basic computer proficiency. * On occasion there might be a need to do a van delivery so a full clean B driver’s license would be an advantage but not essential for this role. * Counterbalance \& Reach Forklift Certificate would be an advantage but not essential for this role. **Additional Information:** * We have one shift available \- 8am to 5pm. * Competitive salary starting from €29,000 per annum depending on experience. * Conveniently located near the Luas line at Cookstown station (5 minutes walk from the warehouse). Also conveniently located near the M50\. **Working Hours** * Monday to Friday business hours. * Occasionally Saturday or extended hours during busy periods so some flexibility will be required. This is a permanent position and will be based at our Cookstown Industrial Estate, Tallaght warehouse, Dublin. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €29,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Wellness program Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Wastewater Modellers - Dublin, Belfast, Cork
description: At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission\-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting\-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good. Your impact Join Our Team as a Wastewater Modeller! \- Dublin, Cork, Belfast. Join Jacobs at a pivotal moment in the Irish water sector, where investment is at record levels and the opportunity to shape transformative infrastructure is real. As a Wastewater Modeller, your expertise will directly influence the success of major water programmes that improve communities and protect the environment. At Jacobs, you’ll be part of a collaborative team working with Ireland’s largest water companies—from early feasibility and planning through to design, construction, and long\-term operations. Your modelling insights will help drive smarter, more sustainable solutions across Ireland. We’re looking for professionals who are ready to take the next step in their career—those who want to be challenged, supported, and empowered to make a difference. Whether you're refining hydraulic models, supporting flood risk assessments, or contributing to climate resilience strategies, your work will matter. **Responsibilities** * Support technical delivery and assist the Project Manager to ensure successful delivery of robustly defined technical solutions. * Experience in network hydraulic modelling (InfoWorks ICM). * Work as part of a team on a variety of projects including model build and verification, using existing models to understand system performance. * Optioneering and solutions development for flooding and CSO drivers. * Interact directly with various personnel in our client’s organisations, including face\-to\-face progress meetings. * Investigate, analyse, and resolve drainage network issues. * Scope network surveys to supplement model updates. * Review and manage weekly contractor survey submissions. * Build and maintain hydraulic models. * Deliver technical reports and hydraulic modelling studies. * Develop modelled engineering solutions to mitigate development impacts, solve sewer flooding, and address unsatisfactory overflows. * Assist with preparation of cost estimates for modelling. * Supervise site surveys and field investigations. * Plan, programme, co\-ordinate, and write reports for specific projects. * Provide guidance and support to graduate engineers or technicians. Here's what you'll need * Proficiency in wastewater modelling software such as InfoAsset Manager, and InfoWorks ICM * Working knowledge of GIS tools (MapInfo, ArcGIS) * Ability to work to client specifications and industry standards * Demonstrable track record of technical achievement and client satisfaction within the water industry * Capable of building and maintaining strong relationships with internal staff and external clients * Ability to be flexible and to be able to multi\-task and prioritise when necessary \#WasteWaterIreland Joining Jacobs not only connects you locally but globally. Our values stand on a foundation of safety, integrity, inclusion and belonging. We put people at the heart of our business, and we truly believe that by supporting one another through our culture of caring, we all succeed. We value positive mental health and a sense of belonging for all employees. With safety and flexibility always top of mind, we’ve gone beyond traditional ways of working so you have the support, means and space to maximize your potential. You’ll uncover flexible working arrangements, benefits, and opportunities, from well\-being benefits to our global giving and volunteering program, to exploring new and inventive ways to help our clients make the world a better place. No matter what drives you, you’ll discover how you can cultivate, nurture, and achieve your goals – all at a single global company. Find out more about life at Jacobs. We aim to embed inclusion and belonging in everything we do. We know that if we are inclusive, we’re more connected and creative. We accept people for who they are, and we champion the richness of different perspectives, lived experiences and backgrounds in the workplace, as a source of learning and innovation. We are committed to building vibrant communities within Jacobs, including through our Jacobs Employee Networks, Communities of Practice and our Find Your Community initiatives, allowing every employee to find connection, purpose, and belonging. Find out more about our Jacobs Employee Networks here. Jacobs partners with VERCIDA to help us attract and retain talent from a wide range of backgrounds. For greater online accessibility please visit https//www.vercida.com/uk/employers/jacobs to view and access our roles. As a disability confident employer, we will interview disabled candidates who best meet the criteria. We welcome applications from candidates who are seeking flexible working and from those who may not meet all the listed requirements for a role. We value collaboration and believe that in\-person interactions are crucial for both our culture and client delivery. We empower employees with our hybrid working policy, allowing them to split their work week between Jacobs offices/projects and remote locations enabling them to deliver their best work. Your application experience is important to us, and we’re keen to adapt to make every interaction even better. If you require further support or reasonable adjustments with regards to the recruitment process (for example, you require the application form in a different format), please contact the team via Careers Support.

title: Workforce Management Specialist (Monetisation Integrity Ops)
description: **Responsibilities** About the team The MI Workforce Management (WFM) team is essential to ensuring our operations are efficiently staffed and strategically aligned with TikTok's growth. As a key partner to Monetization Integrity Ops stakeholders, the Workforce Management Specialist is responsible for planning and optimizing workforce requirements across both in\-house and vendor sites. By collaborating closely with internal teams and leveraging data\-driven insights, you will continually drive improvements in performance, staffing models, and operational efficiency, making a significant impact on the overall success of our platform. Responsibilities: \- Act as key workforce business partner to stakeholders, encompassing planning for all workforce requirements of MI Operations, whether in\-house or vendor sites; ensuring we have the right number of staff available at the right time. \- Partner with internal teams to agree required levels of Auto\-Moderation to optimize human\-review performance for business needs. \- Continually identify forecasting performance gaps and implement improvements. Own and develop OKR's that raise the bar in performance. \- Coordinate with operations teams on HC requirement and cross utilisation opportunities, in the case of unexpected rises in moderation volumes or absenteeism. \- Be responsible for monitoring operational performance to ensure optimum productivity and results, evaluate schedules to meet incoming volumes, and evaluate potential productivity, quality and customer\-service standards by analyzing performance statistics and financial results. \- Drive continuous improvement on WFM processes/tools/systems including streamlining \& optimizing HC allocation and real\-time management. \- Work as the Point of Contact to provide ad\-hoc and regular support to stakeholders \- Deeply interrogate business and operational data to generate strategic, executive level insights on headcount rightsizing, economies of scale and other operations improvement initiatives. **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications \- 5\+ years experience in data, business and/or product analytics or WFM \- Proven experience in business partnering and cross\-functional projects, with a focus on hands\-on capacity planning in a multi\-channel \& multi\-site tech or media company. \- Deep understanding of operational statistics, business dynamics in a service environment, process development and driving operational results with data. \- Expert skills in Excel, advanced in SQL, knowledge \& experience of different forecasting models/methods. Preferred Qualifications \- Knowledge of Business Intelligence tools e.g. Tableau, Power BI. \- Superb written and verbal communication skills, with an ability to craft business messaging appropriately \& concisely for different audiences. \- Experience building reporting \& visualisation dashboards. \- Excellent problem\-solving, task prioritisation, follow\-up, and quick learning skills; Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment with dedication, passion and motivation \- Ads or content moderation experience is a plus. **Trust \& Safety** Content that this role interacts with includes images, video, and text related to every\-day life, but it can also include (but is not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child safety; depictions of harm to self and others, and harm to animals. Hence, it is possible that this role will be exposed to harmful content on a daily basis. ​ ​ TikTok recognises that keeping our platform safe for the TikTok communities is no ordinary job which can be both rewarding and psychologically demanding and emotionally taxing for some. This is why we are sharing the potential hazards, risks and implications in this unique line of work from the start, so our candidates are well informed before joining. ​ ​ We are committed to the wellbeing of all our employees and promise to provide comprehensive and evidence\-based programs, to promote and support physical and mental wellbeing throughout each employee's journey with us. We believe that wellbeing is a relationship and that everyone has a part to play, so we work in collaboration and consultation with our employees and across our functions in order to ensure a truly person\-centred, innovative and integrated approach.​ **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​ **Why Join Us** Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day.​ We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​ ​ Diversity \& Inclusion​ TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​

title: Workforce Management Specialist (Monetisation Integrity Ops)
description: Global Operations Workforce Management Specialist (Monetisation Integrity Ops) Location : Dublin Employment Type : Regular Job Code : A86858A Responsibilities About the team The MI Workforce Management (WFM) team is essential to ensuring our operations are efficiently staffed and strategically aligned with TikTok's growth. As a key partner to Monetization Integrity Ops stakeholders, the Workforce Management Specialist is responsible for planning and optimizing workforce requirements across both in\-house and vendor sites. By collaborating closely with internal teams and leveraging data\-driven insights, you will continually drive improvements in performance, staffing models, and operational efficiency, making a significant impact on the overall success of our platform. **Responsibilities:** * Act as key workforce business partner to stakeholders, encompassing planning for all workforce requirements of MI Operations, whether in\-house or vendor sites; ensuring we have the right number of staff available at the right time. * Partner with internal teams to agree required levels of Auto\-Moderation to optimize human\-review performance for business needs. * Continually identify forecasting performance gaps and implement improvements. Own and develop OKR's that raise the bar in performance. * Coordinate with operations teams on HC requirement and cross utilisation opportunities, in the case of unexpected rises in moderation volumes or absenteeism. * Be responsible for monitoring operational performance to ensure optimum productivity and results, evaluate schedules to meet incoming volumes, and evaluate potential productivity, quality and customer\-service standards by analyzing performance statistics and financial results. * Drive continuous improvement on WFM processes/tools/systems including streamlining \& optimizing HC allocation and real\-time management. * Work as the Point of Contact to provide ad\-hoc and regular support to stakeholders * Deeply interrogate business and operational data to generate strategic, executive level insights on headcount rightsizing, economies of scale and other operations improvement initiatives. Qualifications Minimum Qualifications * 5\+ years experience in data, business and/or product analytics or WFM * Proven experience in business partnering and cross\-functional projects, with a focus on hands\-on capacity planning in a multi\-channel \& multi\-site tech or media company. * Deep understanding of operational statistics, business dynamics in a service environment, process development and driving operational results with data. * Expert skills in Excel, advanced in SQL, knowledge \& experience of different forecasting models/methods. Preferred Qualifications * Knowledge of Business Intelligence tools e.g. Tableau, Power BI. * Superb written and verbal communication skills, with an ability to craft business messaging appropriately \& concisely for different audiences. * Experience building reporting \& visualisation dashboards. * Excellent problem\-solving, task prioritisation, follow\-up, and quick learning skills; Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment with dedication, passion and motivation * Ads or content moderation experience is a plus. Job Information Trust \& Safety Content that this role interacts with includes images, video, and text related to every\-day life, but it can also include (but is not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child safety; depictions of harm to self and others, and harm to animals. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless.
